description: Release of a person accused of or suspected of commission of an offence from the custody of law upon certain conditions imposed by an officer or Court on execution by such person of bond or a bail bond.

title: Bailable offence
description: An offence which is shown as bailable in the First Schedule, or which is made bailable by any other law for the time being in force.

title: Bail bond
description: An undertaking for release with surety.

title: Bond
description: A personal bond or an undertaking for release without surety.

title: Charge
description: Includes any head of charge when the charge contains more heads than one.

title: Cognizable offence
description: An offence for which, and cognizable case means a case in which, police officer may, in accordance with the First Schedule or under any other law for the time being in force, arrest without warrant.

title: Complaint
description: Any allegation made orally or in writing to a Magistrate, with a view to his taking action under this Sanhita, that some person, whether known or unknown, has committed an offence, but does not include a police report.

title: Electronic communication
description: Communication of any written, verbal, pictorial information or video content transmitted by means of an electronic device including telephone, mobile phone, computer, or any other electronic device.

title: High Court
description: In relation to any State, the High Court for that State; in relation to a Union territory, the highest Court of criminal appeal for that territory other than the Supreme Court of India.

title: Inquiry
description: Every inquiry, other than a trial, conducted under this Sanhita by a Magistrate or Court.

title: Investigation
description: All the proceedings under this Sanhita for the collection of evidence conducted by a police officer or by any person authorized by a Magistrate.

title: Judicial proceeding
description: Any proceeding in the course of which evidence is or may be legally taken on oath.

title: Local jurisdiction
description: The local area within which the Court or Magistrate may exercise all or any of its or his powers under this Sanhita.

title: Non-cognizable offence
description: An offence for which, and non-cognizable case means a case in which, a police officer has no authority to arrest without warrant.

title: Notification
description: A notification published in the Official Gazette.

title: Offence
description: Any act or omission made punishable by any law for the time being in force and includes any act in respect of which a complaint may be made under section 20 of the Cattle Trespass Act, 1871.

title: Officer in charge of a police station
description: Includes, when the officer in charge is absent or unable to perform duties, the police officer present who is next in rank and above the rank of constable.

title: Place
description: Includes a house, building, tent, vehicle and vessel.

title: Police report
description: A report forwarded by a police officer to a Magistrate under sub-section (3) of section 193.

title: Police station
description: Any post or place declared generally or specially by the State Government to be a police station.

title: Public Prosecutor
description: Any person appointed under section 18, and includes any person acting under the directions of a Public Prosecutor.

title: Sub-division
description: A sub-division of a district.

title: Summons-case
description: A case relating to an offence, and not being a warrant-case.

title: Victim
description: A person who has suffered any loss or injury caused by reason of the act or omission of the accused person and includes the guardian or legal heir of such victim.

title: Warrant-case
description: A case relating to an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or imprisonment for a term exceeding two years.

title: IT Act and BNS definitions
description: Words and expressions used but not defined in this Sanhita but defined in the Information Technology Act, 2000 and the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 shall have the meanings assigned in those Acts.

title: Construction of references
description: Any reference in any law to a Magistrate shall be construed as a reference to a Judicial Magistrate of the first class or second class exercising jurisdiction in such area.

title: Reference to Magistrate
description: Unless the context otherwise requires, any reference to a Magistrate shall be a reference to a Judicial Magistrate of the first class or second class.

title: Functions of Magistrate
description: Functions involving appreciation of evidence shall be exercisable by a Judicial Magistrate; administrative functions shall be exercisable by an Executive Magistrate.

title: Trial of offences under BNS and other laws
description: All offences under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 shall be investigated, inquired into, tried, and otherwise dealt with according to the provisions hereinafter contained.

title: Offences under BNS
description: All offences under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 shall be investigated, inquired into, tried, and otherwise dealt with according to provisions of this Sanhita.

title: Offences under other laws
description: All offences under any other law shall be investigated, inquired into, tried, and otherwise dealt with according to the same provisions, but subject to any special enactment.

title: Saving
description: Nothing contained in this Sanhita shall, in the absence of a specific provision to the contrary, affect any special or local law for the time being in force, or any special jurisdiction or power conferred.

title: Classes of Criminal Courts
description: Besides the High Courts, there shall be in every State: Courts of Session; Judicial Magistrates of the first class and second class; Executive Magistrates.

title: Courts of Session
description: Courts of Session as a class of Criminal Courts.

title: Judicial Magistrates of the first class
description: Judicial Magistrates of the first class as a class of Criminal Courts.

title: Judicial Magistrates of the second class
description: Judicial Magistrates of the second class as a class of Criminal Courts.

title: Executive Magistrates
description: Executive Magistrates as a class of Criminal Courts.

title: Territorial divisions
description: Every State shall be a sessions division or shall consist of sessions divisions; and every sessions division shall be a district or consist of districts.

title: Sessions divisions
description: Every State shall be a sessions division or shall consist of sessions divisions; every sessions division shall be a district or consist of districts.

title: Alteration of limits
description: The State Government may, after consultation with the High Court, alter the limits or the number of such divisions and districts.

title: Sub-divisions
description: The State Government may, after consultation with the High Court, divide any district into sub-divisions and may alter the limits or the number of such sub-divisions.

title: Existing divisions
description: The sessions divisions, districts and sub-divisions existing in a State at the commencement of this Sanhita shall be deemed to have been formed under this section.

title: Court of Session
description: The State Government shall establish a Court of Session for every sessions division. Every Court of Session shall be presided over by a Judge appointed by the High Court.
keywords: Court of Session, Sessions Judge, High Court

title: Establishment of Court of Session
description: The State Government shall establish a Court of Session for every sessions division.

title: Presiding officer
description: Every Court of Session shall be presided over by a Judge, to be appointed by the High Court.

title: Additional Sessions Judges
description: The High Court may also appoint Additional Sessions Judges to exercise jurisdiction in a Court of Session.

title: Sessions Judge of another division
description: The Sessions Judge of one sessions division may be appointed by the High Court to be also an Additional Sessions Judge of another division.

title: Vacancy in office
description: Where the office of the Sessions Judge is vacant, the High Court may make arrangements for disposal of urgent applications by an Additional Sessions Judge or Chief Judicial Magistrate.

title: Place of sitting
description: The Court of Session shall ordinarily hold its sitting at such place as the High Court may specify; but may sit at any other place with consent of prosecution and accused.

title: Distribution of business
description: The Sessions Judge may make orders as to the distribution of business among Additional Sessions Judges.

title: Urgent applications
description: The Sessions Judge may make provision for disposal of urgent applications in event of his absence by an Additional Sessions Judge or Chief Judicial Magistrate.

title: Courts of Judicial Magistrates
description: In every district there shall be established Courts of Judicial Magistrates of the first class and of the second class, at such places as the State Government may specify.
keywords: Judicial Magistrates, first class, second class

title: Establishment
description: In every district there shall be established as many Courts of Judicial Magistrates of the first class and second class, and at such places, as the State Government may, after consultation with the High Court, specify.

title: Presiding officers
description: The presiding officers of such Courts shall be appointed by the High Court.

title: Powers on Civil Court Judge
description: The High Court may confer the powers of a Judicial Magistrate of the first class or second class on any member of the Judicial Service functioning as a Judge in a Civil Court.

title: Chief Judicial Magistrate and Additional Chief Judicial Magistrate
description: In every district, the High Court shall appoint a Judicial Magistrate of the first class to be the Chief Judicial Magistrate.

title: Chief Judicial Magistrate
description: In every district, the High Court shall appoint a Judicial Magistrate of the first class to be the Chief Judicial Magistrate.

title: Additional Chief Judicial Magistrate
description: The High Court may appoint any Judicial Magistrate of the first class to be an Additional Chief Judicial Magistrate with all or any powers of a CJM.

title: Sub-divisional Judicial Magistrate
description: The High Court may designate any Judicial Magistrate of the first class in any sub-division as the Sub-divisional Judicial Magistrate.

title: Powers of SDJM
description: Subject to the general control of the CJM, every Sub-divisional Judicial Magistrate shall have and exercise such powers of supervision and control over Judicial Magistrates in the sub-division as the High Court may specify.

title: Special Judicial Magistrates
description: The High Court may, if requested by the Central or State Government, confer upon any person who holds or has held any post under the Government, all or any of the powers of a Judicial Magistrate.

title: Conferment of powers
description: The High Court may confer upon any person who holds or has held any post under Government, powers of a Judicial Magistrate of first or second class, in respect to particular cases or classes of cases.

title: Term of appointment
description: Such Magistrates shall be called Special Judicial Magistrates and shall be appointed for such term, not exceeding one year at a time, as the High Court may direct.
keywords: term, one year, Special Judicial Magistrate

title: Local Jurisdiction of Judicial Magistrates
description: Subject to the control of the High Court, the Chief Judicial Magistrate may define the local limits of the areas within which the Magistrates may exercise their powers.

title: Definition of local limits
description: Subject to the control of the High Court, the Chief Judicial Magistrate may, from time to time, define the local limits of the areas within which the Magistrates appointed under section 9 or 11 may exercise their powers.

title: Extent of jurisdiction
description: Except as otherwise provided by such definition, the jurisdiction and powers of every such Magistrate shall extend throughout the district.

title: Extended jurisdiction
description: Where local jurisdiction extends beyond the district, references to Court of Session or CJM shall be construed as reference to those exercising jurisdiction in relation to said district.

title: Subordination of Judicial Magistrates
description: Every Chief Judicial Magistrate shall be subordinate to the Sessions Judge; and every other Judicial Magistrate shall be subordinate to the Chief Judicial Magistrate.

title: Subordination
description: Every Chief Judicial Magistrate shall be subordinate to the Sessions Judge; and every other Judicial Magistrate shall, subject to the general control of the Sessions Judge, be subordinate to the Chief Judicial Magistrate.

title: Distribution of business
description: The Chief Judicial Magistrate may, from time to time, make rules or give special orders as to the distribution of business among the Judicial Magistrates subordinate to him.

title: Executive Magistrates
description: In every district, the State Government may appoint as many persons as it thinks fit to be Executive Magistrates and shall appoint one of them to be the District Magistrate.

title: Appointment
description: In every district, the State Government may appoint as many persons as it thinks fit to be Executive Magistrates and shall appoint one of them to be the District Magistrate.

title: Additional District Magistrate
description: The State Government may appoint any Executive Magistrate to be an Additional District Magistrate with such powers of a District Magistrate as may be directed.

title: Temporary vacancy
description: When the office of District Magistrate becomes vacant, the officer succeeding temporarily to the executive administration shall exercise all powers of the District Magistrate.

title: Sub-divisional Magistrate
description: The State Government may place an Executive Magistrate in charge of a sub-division; such Magistrate shall be called the Sub-divisional Magistrate.

title: Delegation of powers
description: The State Government may delegate its powers under sub-section (4) to the District Magistrate.

title: Commissioner of Police
description: Nothing in this section shall preclude the State Government from conferring on a Commissioner of Police all or any of the powers of an Executive Magistrate.
keywords: Commissioner of Police, Executive Magistrate powers

title: Special Executive Magistrates
description: The State Government may appoint, for such term as it may think fit, Executive Magistrates or any police officer not below the rank of Superintendent of Police to be known as Special Executive Magistrates.

title: Local Jurisdiction of Executive Magistrates
description: Subject to the control of the State Government, the District Magistrate may define the local limits of the areas within which Executive Magistrates may exercise their powers.

title: Definition of local limits
description: Subject to the control of the State Government, the District Magistrate may, from time to time, define the local limits of the areas within which the Executive Magistrates may exercise their powers.

title: Extent of jurisdiction
description: Except as otherwise provided by such definition, the jurisdiction and powers of every such Magistrate shall extend throughout the district.

title: Subordination of Executive Magistrates
description: All Executive Magistrates shall be subordinate to the District Magistrate; every Executive Magistrate exercising powers in a sub-division shall also be subordinate to the Sub-divisional Magistrate.

title: Hierarchy
description: All Executive Magistrates shall be subordinate to the District Magistrate, and every Executive Magistrate (other than the SDM) exercising powers in a sub-division shall also be subordinate to the SDM.

title: Distribution of business
description: The District Magistrate may make rules or give special orders as to the distribution or allocation of business among the Executive Magistrates subordinate to him.

title: Public Prosecutors
description: For every High Court, the Central Government or the State Government shall, after consultation with the High Court, appoint a Public Prosecutor and may also appoint Additional Public Prosecutors.

title: PP for High Court
description: For every High Court, the Central or State Government shall, after consultation with the High Court, appoint a Public Prosecutor and may appoint one or more Additional Public Prosecutors.

title: Central Government PP
description: The Central Government may appoint one or more Public Prosecutors for the purpose of conducting any case in any district or local area.

title: PP for district
description: For every district, the State Government shall appoint a Public Prosecutor and may also appoint one or more Additional Public Prosecutors for the district.

title: Panel of names
description: The District Magistrate shall, in consultation with the Sessions Judge, prepare a panel of names of persons who are fit to be appointed as Public Prosecutors or Additional Public Prosecutors.

title: Appointment from panel
description: No person shall be appointed by the State Government as the Public Prosecutor or Additional Public Prosecutor for the district unless his name appears in the panel prepared by the District Magistrate.

title: Regular Cadre of Prosecuting Officers
description: Where a State has a regular Cadre of Prosecuting Officers, the State Government shall appoint a Public Prosecutor only from among persons constituting such Cadre.

title: Eligibility - 7 years practice
description: A person shall be eligible to be appointed as a Public Prosecutor or Additional Public Prosecutor only if he has been in practice as an advocate for not less than seven years.

title: Special Public Prosecutor
description: The Central or State Government may appoint, for any case or class of cases, a person who has been in practice as an advocate for not less than ten years as a Special Public Prosecutor.

title: Deemed practice period
description: The period during which a person has rendered service as a Public Prosecutor or Assistant Public Prosecutor shall be deemed to be the period during which such person has been in practice as an advocate.

title: Assistant Public Prosecutors
description: The State Government shall appoint in every district one or more Assistant Public Prosecutors for conducting prosecutions in the Courts of Magistrates.

title: State Government appointment
description: The State Government shall appoint in every district one or more Assistant Public Prosecutors for conducting prosecutions in the Courts of Magistrates.

title: Central Government appointment
description: The Central Government may appoint one or more Assistant Public Prosecutors for the purpose of conducting any case or class of cases in the Courts of Magistrates.

title: Appointment by DM
description: Where no Assistant Public Prosecutor is available, the District Magistrate may appoint any other person to be the Assistant Public Prosecutor after giving notice of fourteen days to the State Government.

title: Directorate of Prosecution
description: The State Government may establish a Directorate of Prosecution in the State consisting of a Director of Prosecution and Deputy Directors of Prosecution.
keywords: Directorate of Prosecution, Director, Deputy Director

title: Establishment
description: The State Government may establish a Directorate of Prosecution in the State and a District Directorate of Prosecution in every district.

title: Eligibility
description: A Director of Prosecution or Deputy Director must have been in practice as an advocate for not less than fifteen years or is or has been a Sessions Judge; Assistant Director must have seven years practice or been a Magistrate.

title: Administrative control
description: The Directorate of Prosecution shall be headed by the Director of Prosecution, who shall function under the administrative control of the Home Department in the State.

title: Subordination
description: Every Deputy Director or Assistant Director of Prosecution shall be subordinate to the Director of Prosecution.

title: Subordination of PP in High Court
description: Every Public Prosecutor and Additional Public Prosecutor appointed to conduct cases in the High Court shall be subordinate to the Director of Prosecution.

title: Subordination of PP in district
description: Every Public Prosecutor, Additional Public Prosecutor and Special Public Prosecutor in district courts and every Assistant Public Prosecutor shall be subordinate to the Deputy Director or Assistant Director.

title: Functions of Director
description: The powers and functions of the Director of Prosecution shall be to monitor cases punishable for ten years or more, or with life imprisonment, or with death; to expedite proceedings and give opinion on filing of appeals.

title: Functions of Deputy Director
description: The powers and functions of the Deputy Director shall be to examine and scrutinise police report and monitor cases punishable for seven years or more, but less than ten years.

title: Functions of Assistant Director
description: The functions of the Assistant Director shall be to monitor cases punishable for less than seven years.

title: General powers
description: Notwithstanding anything in sub-sections (7), (8) and (9), the Director, Deputy Director or Assistant Director shall have the power to deal with all proceedings under this Sanhita.

title: Other powers
description: The other powers and functions of the Director, Deputy Directors and Assistant Directors of Prosecution shall be such as the State Government may, by notification, specify.

title: Advocate General exemption
description: The provisions of this section shall not apply to the Advocate General for the State while performing the functions of a Public Prosecutor.

title: Courts by which offences are triable
description: Any offence under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 may be tried by the High Court, the Court of Session, or any other Court by which such offence is shown in the First Schedule to be triable.

title: Offences under BNS
description: Any offence under the BNS may be tried by the High Court, Court of Session, or any other Court shown in First Schedule. Offences under sections 64-71 of BNS shall be tried by a Court presided over by a woman.

title: Offences under other laws
description: Any offence under any other law shall, when any Court is mentioned in such law, be tried by such Court; when no Court is mentioned, may be tried by the High Court or Court shown in First Schedule.

title: Sentences which High Courts and Sessions Judges may pass
description: A High Court may pass any sentence authorised by law. A Sessions Judge or Additional Sessions Judge may pass any sentence authorised by law; but any sentence of death shall be subject to confirmation by the High Court.
keywords: High Court, Sessions Judge, sentence, death confirmation

title: High Court
description: A High Court may pass any sentence authorised by law.

title: Sessions Judge
description: A Sessions Judge or Additional Sessions Judge may pass any sentence authorised by law; but any sentence of death passed by any such Judge shall be subject to confirmation by the High Court.

title: Sentences which Magistrates may pass
description: Chief Judicial Magistrate may pass any sentence except death, life imprisonment, or imprisonment exceeding seven years. Magistrate of first class may pass up to 3 years or fine up to Rs. 50,000. Magistrate of second class up to 1 year or Rs. 10,000.

title: Chief Judicial Magistrate
description: The Court of a Chief Judicial Magistrate may pass any sentence authorised by law except a sentence of death or of imprisonment for life or of imprisonment for a term exceeding seven years.

title: Magistrate of the first class
description: The Court of a Magistrate of the first class may pass a sentence of imprisonment for a term not exceeding three years, or of fine not exceeding fifty thousand rupees, or of both, or of community service.

title: Magistrate of the second class
description: The Court of Magistrate of the second class may pass a sentence of imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year, or of fine not exceeding ten thousand rupees, or of both, or of community service.

title: Sentence of imprisonment in default of fine
description: The Court of a Magistrate may award such term of imprisonment in default of payment of fine as is authorised by law.

title: Default imprisonment
description: The Court of a Magistrate may award such term of imprisonment in default of payment of fine as is authorised by law, provided the term is not in excess of the powers of the Magistrate under section 23.

title: Addition to substantive sentence
description: The imprisonment awarded under this section may be in addition to a substantive sentence of imprisonment for the maximum term awardable by the Magistrate under section 23.

title: Sentence in cases of conviction of several offences at one trial
description: When a person is convicted at one trial of two or more offences, the Court may sentence him for such offences to the several punishments and order such punishments to run concurrently or consecutively.

title: Several offences
description: When a person is convicted at one trial of two or more offences, the Court may, subject to section 9 of BNS, sentence him for such offences, and order such punishments to run concurrently or consecutively.

title: Consecutive sentences
description: In the case of consecutive sentences, it shall not be necessary to send the offender for trial before a higher Court; provided that imprisonment shall not exceed twenty years and aggregate punishment shall not exceed twice the maximum for a single offence.

title: Single sentence for appeal
description: For the purpose of appeal by a convicted person, the aggregate of the consecutive sentences passed against him shall be deemed to be a single sentence.

title: Mode of conferring powers
description: In conferring powers under this Sanhita, the High Court or the State Government may empower persons specially by name or in virtue of their offices or classes of officials by their official titles.
keywords: conferring powers, High Court, State Government

title: Powers of officers appointed
description: When a person invested with powers under this Sanhita is appointed to an equal or higher office of the same nature within a like local area, he shall exercise the same powers in the local area in which he is so appointed.

title: Withdrawal of powers
description: The High Court or the State Government may withdraw all or any of the powers conferred under this Sanhita on any person or by any officer subordinate to it.

title: High Court or State Government
description: The High Court or the State Government may withdraw all or any of the powers conferred by it under this Sanhita on any person or by any officer subordinate to it.

title: CJM or DM
description: Any powers conferred by the Chief Judicial Magistrate or by the District Magistrate may be withdrawn by the respective Magistrate by whom such powers were conferred.

title: Powers of Judges and Magistrates exercisable by successors-in-office
description: Subject to the other provisions of this Sanhita, the powers and duties of a Judge or Magistrate may be exercised or performed by his successor-in-office.

title: Exercise by successor
description: Subject to the other provisions of this Sanhita, the powers and duties of a Judge or Magistrate may be exercised or performed by his successor-in-office.

title: Sessions Judge determination
description: When there is any doubt as to who is the successor-in-office, the Sessions Judge shall determine by order in writing the Judge who shall be deemed to be the successor-in-office.

title: CJM or DM determination
description: When there is any doubt as to who is the successor-in-office of any Magistrate, the Chief Judicial Magistrate, or the District Magistrate, as the case may be, shall determine by order in writing.

title: Powers of superior officers of police
description: Police officers superior in rank to an officer in charge of a police station may exercise the same powers, throughout the local area to which they are appointed, as may be exercised by such officer within the limits of his station.

title: Public when to assist Magistrates and police
description: Every person is bound to assist a Magistrate or police officer reasonably demanding his aid in the taking or preventing escape of any person, in prevention of breach of peace, or prevention of injury to public property.

title: Preventing escape
description: Every person is bound to assist in the taking or preventing the escape of any other person whom such Magistrate or police officer is authorised to arrest.

title: Breach of peace
description: Every person is bound to assist in the prevention or suppression of a breach of the peace.

title: Public property
description: Every person is bound to assist in the prevention of any injury attempted to be committed to any public property.

title: Aid to person executing warrant
description: When a warrant is directed to a person other than a police officer, any other person may aid in the execution of such warrant, if the person to whom the warrant is directed be near at hand and acting in execution.

title: Public to give information of certain offences
description: Every person aware of the commission of, or intention to commit, certain specified offences shall forthwith give information to the nearest Magistrate or police officer.

title: Specified offences
description: Every person aware of commission of, or intention to commit, offences under BNS sections 103-105, 111-113, 140-144, 147-154, 158, 178-182, 189, 191, 274-280, 307, 309-312, 316(5), 326-328, 331, 332 shall give information.

title: Offences outside India
description: For the purposes of this section, the term 'offence' includes any act committed at any place out of India which would constitute an offence if committed in India.

title: Duty of village officers to report
description: Every officer employed in connection with affairs of a village and every person residing in a village shall forthwith communicate certain information to the nearest Magistrate or police station.

title: Information to communicate
description: Information about notorious receivers of stolen property, robbers, escaped convicts, proclaimed offenders, non-bailable offences, sudden or unnatural deaths, and certain offences outside India.

title: Definitions
description: In this section, 'village' includes village lands; 'proclaimed offender' includes any person proclaimed as an offender by any Court or authority; 'officer employed in connection with affairs of village' means a member of the panchayat.

title: When police may arrest without warrant
description: Any police officer may without an order from a Magistrate and without a warrant, arrest any person in specified circumstances including cognizable offences, proclaimed offenders, stolen property possession.

title: Conditions for arrest
description: Police may arrest: (a) person committing cognizable offence in presence; (b) against whom reasonable complaint or credible information exists for cognizable offence up to 7 years; (c) against whom credible information of offence over 7 years exists.

title: Offence in presence
description: Who commits, in the presence of a police officer, a cognizable offence.

title: Offence up to 7 years
description: Against whom a reasonable complaint has been made, or credible information received, or reasonable suspicion exists that he has committed a cognizable offence punishable with imprisonment up to seven years.

title: Offence over 7 years
description: Against whom credible information has been received that he has committed a cognizable offence punishable with imprisonment exceeding seven years or with death sentence.

title: Proclaimed offender
description: Who has been proclaimed as an offender either under this Sanhita or by order of the State Government.

title: Stolen property
description: In whose possession anything is found which may reasonably be suspected to be stolen property and who may reasonably be suspected of having committed an offence with reference to such thing.

title: Obstruction or escape
description: Who obstructs a police officer while in the execution of his duty, or who has escaped, or attempts to escape, from lawful custody.

title: Deserter
description: Who is reasonably suspected of being a deserter from any of the Armed Forces of the Union.

title: Extradition
description: Who has been concerned in any act committed outside India which, if committed in India, would have been punishable as an offence, and for which he is liable to be apprehended under any law relating to extradition.

title: Released convict breach
description: Who, being a released convict, commits a breach of any rule made under sub-section (5) of section 394.

title: Requisition from another officer
description: For whose arrest any requisition has been received from another police officer specifying the person and offence for which arrest is to be made.

title: Non-cognizable offence
description: No person concerned in a non-cognizable offence shall be arrested except under a warrant or order of a Magistrate.

title: Notice to appear
description: Where arrest is not required, police officer shall issue a notice directing the person to appear before him or at such other place as may be specified.

title: Duty to comply
description: Where such a notice is issued to any person, it shall be the duty of that person to comply with the terms of the notice.

title: No arrest on compliance
description: Where such person complies and continues to comply with the notice, he shall not be arrested in respect of the offence referred to in the notice unless the police officer records reasons.

title: Failure to comply
description: Where such person fails to comply with the terms of the notice or is unwilling to identify himself, the police officer may arrest him for the offence mentioned in the notice.

title: Prior permission for certain arrests
description: No arrest shall be made without prior permission of an officer not below the rank of Deputy Superintendent of Police in case of an offence punishable for less than three years and such person is infirm or above sixty years of age.

title: Procedure of arrest and duties of officer making arrest
description: Every police officer while making an arrest shall bear visible identification, prepare a memorandum of arrest attested by witness, and inform the person of right to inform a relative or friend.

title: Identification
description: Every police officer while making an arrest shall bear an accurate, visible and clear identification of his name which will facilitate easy identification.

title: Memorandum of arrest
description: Prepare a memorandum of arrest which shall be attested by at least one witness (family member or respectable member of locality) and countersigned by the person arrested.

title: Right to inform
description: Inform the person arrested, unless the memorandum is attested by a member of his family, that he has a right to have a relative or friend or any other person named by him to be informed of his arrest.

title: Designated police officer
description: The State Government shall establish a police control room in every district and at State level; designate a police officer in every district and police station not below the rank of ASI to maintain arrest information.

title: Police control room
description: The State Government shall establish a police control room in every district and at State level.

title: Information maintenance
description: Designate a police officer in every district and police station, not below ASI rank, to maintain information about names, addresses, nature of offence of arrested persons, displayed prominently including digitally.

title: Right of arrested person to meet an advocate
description: When any person is arrested and interrogated by the police, he shall be entitled to meet an advocate of his choice during interrogation, though not throughout interrogation.

title: Arrest on refusal to give name and residence
description: When any person who has committed or been accused of a non-cognizable offence refuses on demand of a police officer to give his name and residence, he may be arrested to ascertain the same.

title: Arrest for ascertaining name
description: When any person in the presence of a police officer who has committed or been accused of a non-cognizable offence refuses to give name and residence, or gives a false name or residence, he may be arrested.

title: Release on ascertaining
description: When the true name and residence of such person have been ascertained, he shall be released on a bond or bail bond, to appear before a Magistrate if required.

title: Forwarding to Magistrate
description: If the true name and residence is not ascertained within twenty-four hours or if he fails to execute the bond or furnish sureties, he shall forthwith be forwarded to the nearest Magistrate.

title: Arrest by private person and procedure
description: Any private person may arrest or cause to be arrested any person who in his presence commits a non-bailable and cognizable offence, or any proclaimed offender, and without unnecessary delay within six hours make over to police.

title: Arrest and handover
description: Any private person may arrest any person who in his presence commits a non-bailable and cognizable offence, or any proclaimed offender, and within six hours make over to a police officer or take to nearest police station.

title: Police custody
description: If there is reason to believe that such person comes under section 35(1), a police officer shall take him in custody.

title: Non-cognizable offence
description: If there is reason to believe he committed a non-cognizable offence and refuses to give name and residence, he shall be dealt with under section 39; if no sufficient reason to believe any offence, he shall be released at once.

title: Arrest by Magistrate
description: When any offence is committed in the presence of a Magistrate within his local jurisdiction, he may himself arrest or order any person to arrest the offender, and commit the offender to custody subject to bail provisions.

title: Offence in presence
description: When any offence is committed in the presence of a Magistrate, whether Executive or Judicial, within his local jurisdiction, he may himself arrest or order any person to arrest the offender.

title: Warrant competence
description: Any Magistrate may at any time arrest or direct the arrest, in his presence, within his local jurisdiction, of any person for whose arrest he is competent to issue a warrant.

title: Protection of members of Armed Forces from arrest
description: No member of the Armed Forces of the Union shall be arrested for anything done or purported to be done in the discharge of official duties except after obtaining the consent of the Central Government.
keywords: Armed Forces, protection, Central Government consent

title: Consent required
description: Notwithstanding anything in sections 35, 39-41, no member of the Armed Forces of the Union shall be arrested for anything done in discharge of official duties except after obtaining consent of Central Government.

title: State Government application
description: The State Government may direct that the provisions of sub-section (1) shall apply to members of the Force charged with maintenance of public order.

title: Arrest how made
description: In making an arrest the police officer shall actually touch or confine the body of the person to be arrested, unless there be a submission to the custody by word or action.

title: Physical contact
description: In making an arrest, the police officer shall actually touch or confine the body of the person; provided that where a woman is to be arrested, her submission to custody on oral intimation shall be presumed unless circumstances indicate otherwise.

title: Force if resistance
description: If such person forcibly resists the endeavour to arrest him, or attempts to evade the arrest, such police officer may use all means necessary to effect the arrest.

title: Use of handcuff
description: The police officer may use handcuff while arresting a person who is a habitual or repeat offender, escaped from custody, or committed organised crime, terrorist act, drug crime, murder, rape, acid attack, human trafficking, etc.

title: Not causing death
description: Nothing in this section gives a right to cause the death of a person who is not accused of an offence punishable with death or with imprisonment for life.

title: Arrest of woman
description: Save in exceptional circumstances, no woman shall be arrested after sunset and before sunrise, and where such exceptional circumstances exist, the woman police officer shall obtain prior permission of the first class Magistrate.

title: Search of place entered by person sought to be arrested
description: If any person acting under a warrant of arrest or police officer has reason to believe that the person to be arrested is within any place, the person in charge of such place shall allow ingress and facilities for search.

title: Free ingress
description: If any person acting under a warrant of arrest, or police officer having authority to arrest, has reason to believe that the person to be arrested is within any place, the person in charge shall allow free ingress.

title: Breaking open
description: If ingress cannot be obtained, it shall be lawful for a person acting under a warrant to enter such place and break open any outer or inner door or window after notification of authority and purpose.

title: Liberation
description: Any police officer or other person authorised to make an arrest may break open any door or window to liberate himself or any other person who, having lawfully entered for arrest, is detained therein.

title: Pursuit of offenders into other jurisdictions
description: A police officer may, for the purpose of arresting without warrant any person whom he is authorised to arrest, pursue such person into any place in India.

title: No unnecessary restraint
description: The person arrested shall not be subjected to more restraint than is necessary to prevent his escape.

title: Person arrested to be informed of grounds of arrest and right to bail
description: Every police officer or other person arresting any person without warrant shall forthwith communicate to him full particulars of the offence and other grounds for such arrest.
keywords: grounds of arrest, bail right, communication

title: Grounds of arrest
description: Every police officer or other person arresting any person without warrant shall forthwith communicate to him full particulars of the offence for which he is arrested or other grounds for such arrest.

title: Right to bail
description: Where a police officer arrests without warrant any person other than a person accused of a non-bailable offence, he shall inform the person arrested that he is entitled to be released on bail and may arrange for sureties.

title: Obligation to inform about arrest to relative or friend
description: Every police officer making any arrest shall forthwith give information regarding such arrest and place where the arrested person is being held to any relative, friend or person nominated by the arrested person.

title: Information to relatives
description: Every police officer making any arrest shall forthwith give information regarding such arrest and place where the arrested person is being held to any relative, friend, or nominated person, and also to the designated police officer.

title: Inform arrested person
description: The police officer shall inform the arrested person of his rights under sub-section (1) as soon as he is brought to the police station.

title: Entry in book
description: An entry of the fact as to who has been informed of the arrest shall be made in a book to be kept in the police station in such form as the State Government may provide.

title: Magistrate satisfaction
description: It shall be the duty of the Magistrate before whom such arrested person is produced, to satisfy himself that the requirements of sub-section (2) and (3) have been complied with.

title: Search of arrested person
description: The police officer making the arrest may search such person, and place in safe custody all articles, other than necessary wearing-apparel, found upon him and give a receipt for articles seized.

title: Search and custody
description: When a person is arrested and cannot be admitted to bail or is unable to furnish bail, the officer making the arrest may search such person and place in safe custody all articles other than necessary wearing-apparel.

title: Female search
description: Whenever it is necessary to cause a female to be searched, the search shall be made by another female with strict regard to decency.

title: Power to seize offensive weapons
description: The police officer making any arrest may immediately after the arrest take from the person arrested any offensive weapons and deliver all weapons so taken to the Court or officer before whom the person is to be produced.

title: Examination of accused by medical practitioner at request of police officer
description: When a person is arrested and examination of his person will afford evidence as to the commission of an offence, a registered medical practitioner may make such examination and use force as is reasonably necessary.

title: Examination
description: When there are reasonable grounds for believing that examination of the person arrested will afford evidence as to the commission of an offence, it shall be lawful for a registered medical practitioner to make such examination.

title: Female examination
description: Whenever the person of a female is to be examined under this section, the examination shall be made only by, or under the supervision of, a female registered medical practitioner.

title: Report forwarding
description: The registered medical practitioner shall, without any delay, forward the examination report to the investigating officer.

title: Examination of person accused of rape by medical practitioner
description: When a person is arrested on a charge of rape or attempt to commit rape, a registered medical practitioner shall make examination and prepare a report with specified particulars.

title: Examination for rape offence
description: When a person is arrested on a charge of committing an offence of rape or an attempt to commit rape, a registered medical practitioner employed in a Government hospital may make such an examination.

title: Report particulars
description: The medical practitioner shall prepare a report giving: name and address of accused, age, marks of injury, description of material taken for DNA profiling, and other material particulars.

title: Precise reasons
description: The report shall state precisely the reasons for each conclusion arrived at.

title: Time noted
description: The exact time of commencement and completion of the examination shall also be noted in the report.

title: Report forwarding
description: The registered medical practitioner shall, without any delay, forward the report to the investigating officer, who shall forward it to the Magistrate as part of documents under section 193(6)(a).

title: Examination of arrested person by medical officer
description: When any person is arrested, he shall be examined by a medical officer in the service of the Central or State Government soon after the arrest is made.

title: Examination after arrest
description: When any person is arrested, he shall be examined by a medical officer in the service of the Central or State Government, and in case not available, by a registered medical practitioner soon after the arrest.

title: Record of examination
description: The medical officer or registered medical practitioner shall prepare the record of such examination, mentioning any injuries or marks of violence upon the person arrested, and the approximate time when such injuries may have been inflicted.

title: Identification of person arrested
description: Where a person is arrested and his identification by any other person is considered necessary for investigation, the Court having jurisdiction may direct the person arrested to subject himself to identification.

title: Procedure when police officer deputes subordinate to arrest without warrant
description: When an officer in charge of a police station requires any subordinate officer to arrest without a warrant, he shall deliver to the officer an order in writing specifying the person to be arrested and the offence.

title: Written order
description: When an officer in charge of a police station requires any subordinate officer to arrest without a warrant, he shall deliver a written order specifying the person to be arrested and the offence or other cause.

title: Not affecting section 35
description: Nothing in sub-section (1) shall affect the power of a police officer to arrest a person under section 35.

title: Health and safety of arrested person
description: It shall be the duty of the person having the custody of an accused to take reasonable care of the health and safety of the accused.

title: Person arrested to be taken before Magistrate or officer in charge of police station
description: A police officer making an arrest without warrant shall, without unnecessary delay and subject to provisions as to bail, take or send the person arrested before a Magistrate having jurisdiction or officer in charge of police station.

title: Person arrested not to be detained more than twenty-four hours
description: No person who is arrested shall be detained in custody for a period exceeding twenty-four hours (excluding the time necessary for the journey from the place of arrest to the Magistrate's Court) without being produced before a Magistrate.

title: Police to report apprehensions
description: Officers in charge of police stations shall report to the District Magistrate, or Sub-divisional Magistrate if so directed, the cases of all persons arrested without warrant, whether admitted to bail or otherwise.

title: Discharge of person apprehended
description: No person who has been arrested by a police officer shall be discharged except on his bond, or bail bond, or under the special order of a Magistrate.
keywords: discharge, bond, bail bond, Magistrate order

title: Power, on escape, to pursue and retake
description: If a person in lawful custody escapes or is rescued, the person from whose custody he escaped or was rescued may immediately pursue and arrest him in any place in India.

title: Pursuit and arrest
description: If a person in lawful custody escapes or is rescued, the person from whose custody he escaped or was rescued may immediately pursue and arrest him in any place in India.

title: Section 44 application
description: The provisions of section 44 shall apply to arrests under sub-section (1) although the person making such arrest is not acting under a warrant and is not a police officer having authority to arrest.

title: Arrest to be made strictly according to Sanhita
description: No arrest shall be made except in accordance with the provisions of this Sanhita or any other law for the time being in force providing for arrest.

title: Form of summons
description: Every summons issued by a Court shall be in writing in duplicate, signed by the presiding officer, and shall bear the seal of the Court; or in electronic form with digital signature and image of seal.

title: Summons how served
description: Every summons shall be served by a police officer or officer of the Court. Summons may also be served by electronic communication. Person served shall sign receipt if required.

title: Service of summons on corporate bodies, firms, and societies
description: Service on a company may be effected by serving on Director, Manager, Secretary, or by registered post. Service on a firm may be effected by serving on any partner or by registered post.

title: Service when persons summoned cannot be found
description: Where the person summoned cannot be found, the summons may be served by leaving one of the duplicates with some adult member of his family residing with him.
keywords: cannot be found, adult family member

title: Procedure when service cannot be effected as before provided
description: If service cannot be effected as provided, the serving officer shall affix one of the duplicates to some conspicuous part of the house or homestead where the person summoned ordinarily resides.

title: Service on Government servant
description: Where the person summoned is in the active service of the Government, the Court shall send the summons in duplicate to the head of the office in which such person is employed.

title: Service of summons outside local limits
description: When a Court desires that a summons be served outside its local jurisdiction, it shall ordinarily send such summons in duplicate to a Magistrate within whose local jurisdiction the person summoned resides.

title: Proof of service in such cases
description: When a summons is served outside local jurisdiction, an affidavit purporting to be made before a Magistrate shall be admissible as evidence of service. Electronic summons shall be considered as duly served.

title: Service of summons on witness
description: A Court issuing a summons to a witness may direct a copy to be served by electronic communication or registered post addressed to the witness at his place of residence or business.

title: Form of warrant of arrest and duration
description: Every warrant of arrest issued by a Court shall be in writing, signed by the presiding officer and shall bear the seal of the Court. The warrant shall remain in force until it is cancelled by the Court.

title: Power to direct security to be taken
description: Any Court issuing a warrant for the arrest of any person may in its discretion direct by endorsement on the warrant that, if such person executes a bond with sureties, he shall be released.

title: Warrants to whom directed
description: A warrant of arrest shall ordinarily be directed to one or more police officers; but the Court issuing such a warrant may, if its immediate execution is necessary, direct it to any other person.

title: Warrant may be directed to any person
description: The Chief Judicial Magistrate or a Magistrate of the first class may direct a warrant to any person within his local jurisdiction for the arrest of any escaped convict, proclaimed offender, or person accused of a non-bailable offence.

title: Warrant directed to police officer
description: A warrant directed to any police officer may also be executed by any other police officer whose name is endorsed upon the warrant by the officer to whom it is directed or endorsed.

title: Notification of substance of warrant
description: The police officer or other person executing a warrant of arrest shall notify the substance thereof to the person to be arrested, and, if so required, shall show him the warrant.

title: Person arrested to be brought before Court without delay
description: The police officer or other person executing a warrant of arrest shall bring the person arrested before the Court without unnecessary delay.

title: Where warrant may be executed
description: A warrant of arrest may be executed at any place in India.

title: Warrant forwarded for execution outside jurisdiction
description: When a warrant is to be executed outside the local jurisdiction of the Court issuing it, such Court may forward it to the Executive Magistrate or District Superintendent of Police or Commissioner of Police within whose jurisdiction it is to be executed.

title: Warrant directed to police officer for execution outside jurisdiction
description: When a warrant directed to a police officer is to be executed beyond the local jurisdiction, he shall take it for endorsement to the Executive Magistrate or police officer not below the rank of officer in charge.

title: Procedure on arrest of person against whom warrant issued
description: When a warrant of arrest is executed outside the district in which it was issued, the person arrested shall be taken before the nearest Executive Magistrate or Court unless security is provided.

title: Procedure by Magistrate before whom such person arrested is brought
description: Such Magistrate shall, if the person arrested appears to be the person intended, direct his removal in custody to the Court before which he is required by law to appear.

title: Proclamation for person absconding
description: If a Court has reason to believe that any person against whom a warrant has been issued has absconded or is concealing himself, the Court may publish a written proclamation requiring him to appear.

title: Attachment of property of person absconding
description: The Court issuing a proclamation under section 84 may order the attachment of any property, movable or immovable or both, belonging to the proclaimed person.

title: Identification and attachment of property of proclaimed person
description: The Court may issue a letter of request to the Central Government or the State Government for identification and attachment of property of a proclaimed person through any authority or agency.

title: Claims and objections to attachment
description: Any person whose property has been attached may appear before the Court and claim such property. The Court may release the property if satisfied that the claimant has interest therein.

title: Release, sale and restoration of attached property
description: If the proclaimed person appears within the time specified, the Court shall make an order releasing the property. If he does not appear, the property shall be at the disposal of the State Government.

title: Appeal from order rejecting application for restoration of attached property
description: Any person whose application for release of property has been rejected may, within one year, appeal to the Court to which appeals ordinarily lie from convictions by the Court rejecting the application.

title: Issue of warrant in lieu of, or in addition to, summons
description: A Court may, in any case in which it is empowered to issue a summons for the appearance of any person, issue, after recording its reasons in writing, a warrant for his arrest.
keywords: warrant in lieu of summons, reasons

title: Power to take bond or bail bond for appearance
description: When any person for whose appearance or arrest the officer presiding in any Court is empowered to issue a summons or warrant, is present in such Court, such officer may require such person to execute a bond.

title: Arrest on breach of bond or bail bond for appearance
description: When any person who is bound by any bond to appear before a Court does not so appear, the officer presiding in such Court may issue a warrant directing that such person be arrested and produced.

title: Provisions of this Chapter generally applicable to summons and warrants of arrest
description: The provisions contained in this Chapter relating to a summons and warrant, and their issue, service and execution, shall, so far as may be, apply to every summons and every warrant of arrest issued under this Sanhita.

title: Summons to produce document or other thing
description: Whenever any Court or any officer in charge of a police station considers that the production of any document or other thing is necessary for the purposes of any investigation, inquiry, trial or other proceeding, it may issue a summons.

title: Procedure as to letters
description: If any document in respect of which any offence appears to have been committed is in the custody of the postal or telegraph authority, the Court may require such authority to deliver the document.

title: When search-warrant may be issued
description: Where any Court has reason to believe that a person to whom a summons has been issued will not produce the document or thing, or where such document or thing is not known to be in possession of any person, the Court may issue a search-warrant.

title: Search of place suspected to contain stolen property, forged documents, etc.
description: If a District Magistrate, Sub-divisional Magistrate, or first class Magistrate has reason to believe that any place is used for the deposit or sale of stolen property, he may issue a search-warrant.

title: Power to declare certain publications forfeited and to issue search-warrants for same
description: Where any newspaper, book, or document appears to contain any matter the publication of which is punishable under certain sections of BNS, the State Government may, by notification, declare every copy of the issue to be forfeited.

title: Application to High Court to set aside declaration of forfeiture
description: Any person having any interest in any newspaper, book or other document may apply to the High Court to set aside the declaration of forfeiture within two months from the date of the order.

title: Search for persons wrongfully confined
description: If any District Magistrate, Sub-divisional Magistrate, or first class Magistrate has reason to believe that any person is confined under such circumstances that the confinement amounts to an offence, he may issue a search-warrant.

title: Power to compel restoration of abducted females
description: Upon complaint that any female has been abducted or unlawfully detained, a District Magistrate, Sub-divisional Magistrate, or first class Magistrate may issue a search-warrant and restore her to her liberty.

title: Direction, etc., of search-warrants
description: The Court may specify the particular place to be searched, and the search shall be made in accordance with the provisions of this Sanhita.

title: Persons in charge of closed place to allow search
description: Whenever any place liable to search is closed, any person residing in such place shall, on demand of the officer executing the warrant, allow him free ingress thereto and afford all reasonable facilities for a search.

title: Disposal of things found in search beyond jurisdiction
description: When a police officer in his investigation discovers property beyond his local jurisdiction, he may request the officer in charge of the police station in whose jurisdiction such property is found to seize and detain it.

title: Recording of search and seizure through audio video electronic means
description: The search and seizure shall be recorded through audio video electronic means, and such recording shall be part of the records.

title: Power of police officer to seize certain property
description: Any police officer may seize any property which may be alleged or suspected to have been stolen, or which may be found under circumstances which create suspicion of the commission of any offence.

title: Attachment, forfeiture or restoration of property
description: Where an order of attachment or forfeiture is made by a Court, or property is seized, the Court may direct the manner of disposal or restoration of such property.

title: Magistrate may direct search in his presence
description: Any Magistrate may direct a search to be made in his presence of any place for the search of which he is competent to issue a search-warrant.

title: Power to impound document, etc., produced
description: Any Court may, if it thinks fit, impound any document or thing produced before it under this Sanhita.

title: Reciprocal arrangements regarding processes
description: Where a reciprocal arrangement has been made with any country outside India for the service of summons, warrants and judicial processes, such summons, warrants and processes may be sent to that country.

title: Definitions
description: In this Chapter, 'contracting State' means any country or place outside India in respect of which arrangements have been made by the Central Government with the Government of such country.

title: Letter of request to competent authority for investigation in a country outside India
description: Notwithstanding anything contained in this Sanhita, if an investigation is required to be made in any country outside India, a Court in India may issue a letter of request to a Court or authority in that country.
keywords: letter of request, investigation, outside India

title: Letter of request from a country outside India for investigation in India
description: On receipt of a letter of request from a Court or an authority in a contracting State for investigating an offence, the Central Government may forward the same to the Chief Metropolitan Magistrate or Chief Judicial Magistrate.
keywords: letter of request, investigation in India

title: Assistance in securing transfer of persons
description: Where a letter of request is received from a contracting State for transfer of a person to give evidence in that State, the Central Government may order such transfer after obtaining the consent of such person.

title: Assistance in relation to orders of attachment or forfeiture of property
description: Where a letter of request is received from a contracting State for attachment, seizure, or confiscation of any property in India which has been obtained by commission of an offence in that contracting State.

title: Identifying unlawfully acquired property
description: On receipt of a letter of request from a contracting State, the Court or authority may identify and trace the property alleged or suspected to have been unlawfully acquired.

title: Seizure or attachment of property
description: On identifying the property under section 116, the Court or authority may issue orders for seizing, freezing or attaching such property, as it may deem fit.

title: Management of properties seized or forfeited under this Chapter
description: The Central Government may appoint an administrator for the management of property seized or forfeited under this Chapter, who shall receive and manage such property.

title: Notice of forfeiture of property
description: Where an order of forfeiture has been made by the Court, the administrator shall give notice of such forfeiture to any person whose rights are affected.

title: Forfeiture of property in certain cases
description: Where any property is forfeited under this Chapter, the same shall vest in the Central Government free from all encumbrances.

title: Fine in lieu of forfeiture
description: Where the Court finds it impracticable to forfeit the property, it may impose a fine equal to the value of such property.

title: Certain transfers to be null and void
description: Where any property is seized or attached under this Chapter, any transfer of such property shall be null and void.

title: Procedure in respect of letter of request
description: Every letter of request, summons or warrant received by the Central Government from a contracting State shall be dealt with in accordance with the procedure specified in this Sanhita.

title: Application of this Chapter
description: The provisions of this Chapter shall apply to a contracting State notified by the Central Government, and may be applicable to offences committed before the coming into force of this Chapter.

title: Security for keeping peace on conviction
description: When a Court of Session or Magistrate of the first class convicts a person of certain specified offences or of abetment or attempt of breach of peace, it may require him to execute a bond for keeping the peace for a period not exceeding three years.

title: Security for keeping peace in other cases
description: When an Executive Magistrate receives information that any person is likely to commit a breach of the peace or disturb the public tranquillity, the Magistrate may require such person to show cause why he should not be ordered to execute a bond.
keywords: security, breach of peace, show cause

title: Security for good behaviour from persons disseminating certain matters
description: When an Executive Magistrate receives information that any person is taking part in the publication or circulation of any matter which promotes or is intended to promote feelings of enmity, hatred or ill-will between different classes.

title: Security for good behaviour from suspected persons
description: When an Executive Magistrate receives information that any person is taking precautions to conceal his presence and there is reason to believe that he is doing so with a view to committing a cognizable offence.

title: Security for good behaviour from habitual offenders
description: When an Executive Magistrate receives information that any person within his local jurisdiction is by habit a robber, house-breaker, thief, or forger, or is by habit a receiver of stolen property, or habitually commits certain offences.

title: Order to be made
description: When a Magistrate acting under section 125, 126, 127, 128 or 129 deems it necessary to require any person to show cause, he shall make an order in writing, setting forth the substance of the information received.

title: Procedure in respect of person present in Court
description: If the person in respect of whom such order is made is present in Court, it shall be read over to him, or, if he so requires, the substance thereof shall be explained to him.
keywords: present in Court, read over, explain

title: Summons or warrant in case of person not so present
description: If such person is not present in Court, the Magistrate shall issue a summons requiring him to appear, or, when such person is in custody, a warrant directing the officer in whose custody he is to bring him before the Court.

title: Copy of order to accompany summons or warrant
description: Every summons or warrant issued under section 132 shall be accompanied by a copy of the order made under section 130, and such copy shall be delivered by the officer serving or executing such summons or warrant to the person served or arrested.

title: Power to dispense with personal attendance
description: The Magistrate may, if he sees sufficient cause, dispense with the personal attendance of any person called upon to show cause why he should not be ordered to execute a bond for keeping the peace.

title: Inquiry as to truth of information
description: When an order under section 130 has been read or explained and the person so required shows cause against it, the Magistrate shall proceed to inquire into the truth of the information upon which the action has been taken.

title: Order to give security
description: If, upon such inquiry, it is proved that it is necessary for keeping the peace or maintaining good behaviour that the person in respect of whom the inquiry is made should execute a bond, the Magistrate shall make an order accordingly.

title: Discharge of person informed against
description: If, on an inquiry under section 135, it is not proved that it is necessary for keeping the peace or maintaining good behaviour that the person should execute a bond, the Magistrate shall make an entry on the record to that effect and discharge him.

title: Commencement of period for which security is required
description: If any person in respect of whom an order requiring security is made is, at the time such order is made, sentenced to, or undergoing a sentence of, imprisonment, the period for which such security is required shall commence on the expiration of such sentence.

title: Contents of bond
description: A bond taken under this Chapter shall bind the person to keep the peace or to be of good behaviour, as the case may be, and may, if the Magistrate thinks fit, also bind him not to commit any offence, or not to commit a particular offence.

title: Power to reject sureties
description: A Magistrate may refuse to accept any surety offered, or may reject any surety previously accepted by him or his predecessor, if he is satisfied that such surety is an unfit person.

title: Imprisonment in default of security
description: If any person ordered to give security does not give such security on or before the date on which the period for which such security is to be given commences, he shall be committed to prison.
punishment: Imprisonment up to one year for peace; up to three years for good behaviour

title: Power to release persons imprisoned for failing to give security
description: Whenever the District Magistrate in the case of persons committed by an Executive Magistrate is of opinion that they can be released without hazard to the community, he may order their release.

title: Security for unexpired period of bond
description: When a person who has been ordered to execute a bond under section 136 has, in compliance with such order, furnished security by an approved surety, and such surety subsequently becomes insolvent or dies, he may be required to furnish fresh security.

title: Order for maintenance of wives, children and parents
description: If any person having sufficient means neglects or refuses to maintain his wife, unable to maintain herself, or his legitimate or illegitimate minor child, or his legitimate or illegitimate child who has attained majority and is unable to maintain itself by reason of any physical or mental abnormality or injury, or his father or mother, unable to maintain himself or herself, a Magistrate of the first class may, upon proof of such neglect or refusal, order such person to make a monthly allowance.
punishment: Maintenance allowance as per Court order

title: Procedure
description: Proceedings under section 144 may be taken against any person in any district where he resides or is, or where he last resided with his wife, or where the person claiming maintenance resides.

title: Alteration in allowance
description: On proof of a change in the circumstances of any person, receiving or ordered to pay a monthly allowance, the Magistrate may make such alteration in the allowance as he thinks fit.

title: Enforcement of order of maintenance
description: If any person so ordered fails without sufficient cause to comply with the order, any such Magistrate may, for every breach of the order, issue a warrant for levying the amount due in the manner provided for levying fines, and may sentence such person to imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month.
punishment: Imprisonment up to one month for breach

title: Dispersal of assembly by use of civil force
description: Any Executive Magistrate or officer in charge of a police station may command any unlawful assembly, or any assembly of five or more persons likely to cause a disturbance of the public peace, to disperse.

title: Use of armed forces to disperse assembly
description: If any such assembly cannot be otherwise dispersed, the Executive Magistrate may cause it to be dispersed by the armed forces.

title: Power of certain armed force officers to disperse assembly
description: When the public security is manifestly endangered by any such assembly and no Executive Magistrate can be communicated with, any commissioned or gazetted officer of the armed forces may disperse such assembly.

title: Protection against prosecution for acts done under sections 148, 149 and 150
description: No prosecution shall be instituted in any Criminal Court against any person for any act purporting to be done under section 148, 149 or 150 except with the sanction of the Central Government or State Government.

title: Conditional order for removal of nuisance
description: A District Magistrate or a Sub-divisional Magistrate or any other Executive Magistrate specially empowered may make a conditional order requiring the person causing or likely to cause such obstruction, nuisance or danger to remove the same.

title: Service or notification of order
description: The order shall be served on the person against whom it is made, and if such service cannot reasonably be effected, the order shall be notified by proclamation.

title: Person to whom order is addressed to obey or show cause
description: The person against whom an order under section 152 is made shall obey the order, or appear before the Magistrate and show cause against the same.

title: Penalty for failure to comply with section 154
description: If such person does not perform the act directed by the order, or does not appear and show cause, he shall be liable to the penalty prescribed for disobedience to an order duly promulgated by a public servant.
punishment: Penalty as per BNS Section 223

title: Procedure where existence of public right is denied
description: If the person against whom an order under section 152 is made denies the existence of any public right which forms the basis of the order, the Magistrate shall inquire into the existence of such right.

title: Procedure where person appears to show cause
description: If such person appears and shows cause, the Magistrate shall take evidence in the matter as in a summons-case.

title: Power of Magistrate to direct local investigation and examination of an expert
description: The Magistrate may direct a local investigation to be made by any person whom he thinks fit, or may examine any expert with reference to the matter.

title: Power of Magistrate to furnish written instructions, etc.
description: The Magistrate may furnish written instructions to the person making the investigation or may direct him to make inquiries.

title: Procedure on order being made absolute and consequences of disobedience
description: If the Magistrate is satisfied that the order should be made absolute, he shall make the same absolute; disobedience of such order is punishable under BNS.
punishment: Punishment as per BNS

title: Injunction pending inquiry
description: A Magistrate may by order direct the person against whom the order is made to abstain from any act until the inquiry is concluded.

title: Magistrate may prohibit repetition or continuance of public nuisance
description: A District Magistrate or Sub-divisional Magistrate or any Executive Magistrate empowered by the State Government may, by order, prohibit the repetition or continuance of a public nuisance.

title: Power to issue order in urgent cases of nuisance or apprehended danger
description: In cases where delay would be prejudicial to the public health, safety or convenience, the District Magistrate, Sub-divisional Magistrate, or any Executive Magistrate specially empowered may by a written order direct any person to abstain from certain acts.

title: Procedure where dispute concerning land or water is likely to cause breach of peace
description: When an Executive Magistrate is satisfied that a dispute likely to cause a breach of the peace exists concerning any land or water, he may make an order stating the grounds of his being satisfied and requiring the parties to attend his Court.
keywords: land dispute, water, breach of peace

title: Power to attach subject of dispute and to appoint receiver
description: If the Magistrate considers the case to be one of emergency, he may attach the subject of dispute and appoint a receiver of the produce or profits thereof.

title: Dispute concerning right of use of land or water
