description: When a dispute concerning the right of use of any land or water affects the public peace, an Executive Magistrate may prohibit any interference with such use pending inquiry.

title: Local inquiry
description: The Magistrate may depute any Magistrate subordinate to him to make a local inquiry and report on the facts of the case.

title: Police to prevent cognizable offences
description: Every police officer may interpose for the purpose of preventing, and shall, to the best of his ability, prevent, the commission of any cognizable offence.

title: Information of design to commit cognizable offences
description: Every police officer receiving information of a design to commit any cognizable offence shall communicate such information to the police officer to whom he is subordinate, and to any other officer whose duty it is to prevent or take cognizance of the offence.

title: Arrest to prevent commission of cognizable offences
description: A police officer knowing of a design to commit any cognizable offence may arrest, without orders from a Magistrate and without a warrant, the person so designing, if it appears that the commission of the offence cannot be otherwise prevented.

title: Prevention of injury to public property
description: A police officer may of his own authority interpose to prevent any injury attempted to be committed in his view to any public property.

title: Persons bound to conform to lawful directions of police
description: All persons are bound to conform to the lawful directions of a police officer given in fulfilment of any of his duties under this Chapter.

title: Information in cognizable cases
description: Information may be given "orally or by electronic communication" to a police station. Electronic communication is to be signed within 3 days. A copy of the FIR must be given free of cost to the informant.

title: Information as to non-cognizable cases and investigation of such cases
description: No police officer shall investigate a non-cognizable case without the order of a Magistrate having power to try such case or commit the case for trial.

title: Police officer's power to investigate cognizable case
description: Any officer in charge of a police station may, without the order of a Magistrate, investigate any cognizable case which a Court having jurisdiction over the local area would have power to inquire into or try.

title: Procedure for investigation
description: The investigating officer shall proceed to the spot to investigate the facts and circumstances of the case, and take measures for the discovery and arrest of the offender.

title: Report how submitted
description: The officer in charge of the police station shall, if the information discloses the commission of a cognizable offence, inform the District Magistrate within twenty-four hours of recording the FIR.

title: Power to hold investigation or preliminary inquiry
description: If, from information received or otherwise, an officer in charge of a police station has reason to suspect the commission of an offence, he shall investigate the facts and circumstances of the case and take such measures.

title: Police officer's power to require attendance of witnesses
description: Any police officer making an investigation may, by order in writing, require the attendance before himself of any person within the limits of his own or any adjoining station who appears to be acquainted with the facts.

title: Examination of witnesses by police
description: Any police officer making an investigation may examine orally any person supposed to be acquainted with the facts and circumstances of the case.

title: Statements to police and use thereof
description: No statement made by any person to a police officer in the course of an investigation shall be signed by the person making it. Such statements may be used to contradict the witness if he testifies.

title: No inducement to be offered
description: No police officer or other person in authority shall offer or make any inducement, threat or promise to any person charged with an offence to induce him to make any statement.

title: Recording of confessions and statements
description: Any Metropolitan Magistrate or Judicial Magistrate may, whether or not he has jurisdiction in the case, record any confession or statement made to him in the course of an investigation under this Chapter.

title: Medical examination of victim of rape
description: When a person is arrested on a charge of rape or attempt to rape, the victim shall be examined by a registered medical practitioner employed in a hospital run by the Government, within twenty-four hours.

title: Search by police officer
description: Whenever an officer in charge of a police station has reasonable ground for believing that anything necessary for investigation into any offence may be found in any place, he may search such place.

title: When officer in charge of police station may require another to issue search-warrant
description: An officer in charge of a police station may, by written order, require the officer in charge of another station to cause a search to be made in any place, in his own presence or in the presence of a competent person.

title: Procedure when investigation cannot be completed in twenty-four hours
description: Whenever any person is arrested and detained in custody, and it appears that the investigation cannot be completed within twenty-four hours, and there are grounds for believing that the accusation is well-founded, the officer shall forthwith transmit to the nearest Judicial Magistrate a copy of the entries in the diary and produce the accused before such Magistrate.

title: Report of investigation by subordinate police officer
description: When any subordinate police officer has made any investigation under this Chapter, he shall report the result of such investigation to the officer in charge of the police station.

title: Release of accused when evidence deficient
description: If, upon an investigation under this Chapter, it appears to the officer in charge of the police station that there is not sufficient evidence or reasonable ground, he shall release the accused on his executing a bond.

title: Cases to be sent to Magistrate, when evidence is sufficient
description: If, upon an investigation under this Chapter, it appears to the officer in charge of the police station that there is sufficient evidence, the accused shall be forwarded to a Magistrate empowered to take cognizance of the offence.

title: Complainant and witnesses not to be required to accompany police officer and not to be subject to restraint
description: No complainant or witness on his way to any Court shall be required to accompany a police officer, or shall be subjected to unnecessary restraint or inconvenience.

title: Diary of proceedings in investigation
description: Every police officer making an investigation under this Chapter shall day by day enter his proceedings in the investigation in a diary, setting forth the time at which the information reached him, the time at which he began and closed his investigation.

title: Report of police officer on completion of investigation
description: Every investigation under this Chapter shall be completed without unnecessary delay and within sixty days from the date of recording of the information under section 173 in respect of offences punishable with imprisonment up to three years, and within ninety days for other offences.

title: Completion of investigation
description: Every investigation shall be completed without unnecessary delay - within sixty days for offences punishable up to three years imprisonment, and within ninety days for other offences.

title: Police report to Magistrate
description: On completion of investigation, the officer in charge of the police station shall forward to the Magistrate empowered to take cognizance of the offence a report in the form prescribed by the State Government.

title: Police to enquire and report on suicide, etc.
description: When the officer in charge of a police station receives information that a person has committed suicide, or has been killed by another or by an animal or by machinery, or in a manner raising a reasonable suspicion of commission of an offence, he shall immediately give intimation thereof to the nearest Executive Magistrate.

title: Power to summon persons
description: A police officer proceeding under section 194 may summon two or more persons resident in the neighbourhood to attend and witness the investigation.

title: Inquiry by Magistrate into cause of death
description: When any person dies while in the custody of the police, the nearest Magistrate empowered to hold inquests shall, and in any other case mentioned in section 194 may hold an inquiry into the cause of death.

title: Ordinary place of inquiry and trial
description: Every offence shall ordinarily be inquired into and tried by a Court within whose local jurisdiction it was committed.

title: Place of inquiry or trial
description: The inquiry into or trial of an offence may be held by a Court other than the Court within whose local jurisdiction the offence was committed, in cases where the accused is apprehended or resides in another local area.

title: Offence triable where act is done or consequence ensues
description: When an offence is committed partly in one local area and partly in another, or where the act is done in one local area and the consequence thereof ensues in another local area, the offence may be inquired into or tried in either local area.

title: Place of trial where act is an offence by reason of relation to other offence
description: When an act is an offence by reason of its relation to any other act which is also an offence or which would be an offence if the doer were capable of committing an offence, the first-mentioned offence may be inquired into or tried by a Court within whose local jurisdiction either act was done.

title: Place of trial in case of certain offences
description: Any offence of kidnapping or abducting a person may be inquired into or tried by a Court in whose local area the person was kidnapped or abducted, was conveyed or concealed, or was received or detained.

title: Offences committed by means of electronic communications, letters, etc.
description: Any offence which includes cheating may, if the deception is practised by means of electronic communication, letters, or telecommunication messages, be inquired into or tried by a Court within whose local jurisdiction such communication was sent or received.

title: Offence committed on journey or voyage
description: When an offence is committed whilst the person by or against whom it is committed is in the course of performing a journey or voyage, the offence may be inquired into or tried by a Court through or into whose local jurisdiction that person passed during the journey or voyage.

title: Place of trial for offences triable together
description: When offences are to be tried together, the Court competent to try any of such offences may inquire into or try all of them together.

title: Power to order cases to be tried in different sessions divisions
description: Notwithstanding anything contained in the preceding sections of this Chapter, the State Government may direct that any case or class of cases committed for trial in any district may be tried in any sessions division.

title: High Court to decide, in case of doubt, district where inquiry or trial shall take place
description: Where two or more Courts have taken cognizance of the same offence, and a question arises as to which of them ought to inquire into or try that offence, the question shall be decided by the High Court.

title: Power to issue summons or warrant for offence committed beyond local jurisdiction
description: When a Magistrate of the first class sees reason to believe that any person within his local jurisdiction has committed outside such jurisdiction an offence, he may issue summons or warrant to compel the attendance of such person.

title: Offence committed outside India
description: When an offence is committed outside India by a citizen of India, or by a person on a ship or aircraft registered in India, he may be dealt with in respect of such offence as if it had been committed at any place within India.

title: Receipt of evidence relating to offences committed outside India
description: When any offence alleged to have been committed in a country outside India is being inquired into or tried under the provisions of section 208, the deposition of a witness duly taken in such country may be received as evidence.

title: Cognizance of offences by Magistrate
description: Any Magistrate of the first class, and any Magistrate of the second class specially empowered, may take cognizance of any offence upon receiving a complaint, upon a police report, or upon information received from any person.

title: Transfer on application of accused
description: Whenever it is made to appear to a Court that an order should be made to transfer any particular case, the Court may make an order for the transfer of the case.

title: Making over of cases to Magistrates
description: The Chief Judicial Magistrate may make over, for disposal, any case that has been made over to him to any other Magistrate subordinate to him.

title: Cognizance of offences by Court of Session
description: A Court of Session shall not take cognizance of any offence as a Court of original jurisdiction unless the case has been committed to it by a Magistrate under this Sanhita.

title: Additional Sessions Judges to try cases made over to them
description: An Additional Sessions Judge shall try such cases as the Sessions Judge of the division may, by general or special order, make over to him for trial.

title: Prosecution for contempt of lawful authority of public servants, for offences against public justice and for offences relating to documents given in evidence
description: No Court shall take cognizance of certain offences against public justice or relating to documents given in evidence, except on the complaint in writing of that Court, or of some other Court.

title: Procedure for witnesses in case of threatening, etc.
description: If the Court is satisfied that a witness has been threatened, the Court may take steps to keep his identity secret and may proceed in camera.

title: Prosecution for offences against State and for criminal conspiracy to commit such offence
description: No Court shall take cognizance of an offence punishable under Chapter VII of BNS (offences against State), or of a criminal conspiracy to commit such offence, except with the previous sanction of the Central Government or State Government.
keywords: offences against State, sanction, Central Government

title: Prosecution of Judges and public servants
description: When any person who is or was a Judge or Magistrate or a public servant not removable from his office save by or with the sanction of the Government is accused of any offence alleged to have been committed by him while acting in the discharge of his official duty, no Court shall take cognizance of such offence except with the previous sanction of the Government.

title: Prosecution for offences against marriage
description: No Court shall take cognizance of an offence under section 82, section 83 or section 84 of BNS except upon a complaint made by some person aggrieved by the offence.

title: Prosecution of offences under section 85 of BNS
description: No Court shall take cognizance of an offence punishable under section 85 of BNS (cruelty by husband or relatives) except upon a police report or upon a complaint made by the person aggrieved, or by her father, mother, brother, sister, or her father's or mother's brother or sister.

title: Cognizance of offence
description: No Court shall take cognizance of any offence punishable under sections 195 to 211 of BNS (Perjury, false evidence, etc.) when such offence is alleged to have been committed in, or in relation to, any proceeding in any Court, except on the complaint in writing of such Court.

title: Prosecution for defamation
description: No Court shall take cognizance of an offence falling under Chapter XIX of BNS (Criminal Intimidation, Insult and Defamation) except upon a complaint made by some person aggrieved by the offence.

title: Examination of complainant
description: A Magistrate taking cognizance of an offence on complaint shall examine upon oath the complainant and the witnesses present, if any, and the substance of such examination shall be reduced to writing.

title: Procedure by Magistrate not competent to take cognizance of case
description: If the complaint is made to a Magistrate who is not competent to take cognizance of the offence, he shall return the complaint for presentation to the proper Court.

title: Postponement of issue of process
description: A Magistrate may postpone the issue of process against the accused, and either inquire into the case himself or direct an investigation to be made by a police officer.

title: Dismissal of complaint
description: If, after considering the statements and the result of the investigation or inquiry, the Magistrate is of opinion that there is no sufficient ground for proceeding, he shall dismiss the complaint.

title: Issue of process
description: If in the opinion of the Magistrate taking cognizance of an offence there is sufficient ground for proceeding, and the case appears to be a summons-case, he shall issue his summons; if a warrant-case, he may issue a warrant or a summons.

title: Magistrate may dispense with personal attendance of accused
description: Whenever a Magistrate issues a summons, he may, if he sees reason to do so, dispense with the personal attendance of the accused and permit him to appear by his advocate.

title: Special summons in cases of petty offence
description: If the case relates to an offence punishable only with fine or with imprisonment for a term not exceeding three months, the Magistrate may issue a special summons permitting the accused to plead guilty by post or by advocate.
keywords: petty offence, special summons, plead guilty

title: Supply to accused of copy of police report and other documents
description: In any case where the proceeding has been instituted on a police report, the Magistrate shall supply to the accused free of cost, a copy of the police report and other documents including statements of all witnesses, and the accused shall not be called upon to plead until such copies have been so furnished.
keywords: police report copy, free of cost, documents

title: Supply of copies of statements and documents to accused in other cases triable by Court of Session
description: In cases triable exclusively by the Court of Session, the Magistrate shall supply to the accused free of cost, a copy of the police report and documents.
keywords: Session Court, documents, free of cost

title: Commitment of case to Court of Session when offence is triable exclusively by it
description: If, in a case instituted on a police report or otherwise, the accused appears or is brought before a Magistrate and it appears to the Magistrate that the offence is triable exclusively by the Court of Session, he shall commit the case to that Court.

title: Procedure to be followed when there is a complaint case and police investigation in respect of same offence
description: When in a case instituted otherwise than on a police report, it is made to appear to the Magistrate that an investigation by the police is in progress, the Magistrate may defer the proceedings.

title: Contents of charge
description: Every charge under this Sanhita shall state the offence with which the accused is charged. The charge shall contain particulars as to the time, place and person against whom the offence was committed.

title: Particulars as to time, place and person
description: The charge shall contain such particulars as to the time and place of the alleged offence, and the person (if any) against whom, or the thing (if any) in respect of which, it was committed.

title: When manner of committing offence must be stated
description: When the nature of the case is such that the particulars mentioned in sections 234 and 235 do not give the accused sufficient notice of the matter with which he is charged, the charge shall also contain particulars of the manner in which the alleged offence was committed.

title: Words in charge taken in sense of law under which offence is punishable
description: In every charge words used in describing an offence shall be deemed to have been used in the sense attached to them respectively by the law under which such offence is punishable.

title: Effect of errors
description: No error in stating either the offence or the particulars required to be stated in the charge, and no omission to state the offence or those particulars, shall be regarded at any stage of the case as material.

title: Court may alter charge
description: Any Court may alter or add to any charge at any time before judgment is pronounced.

title: Recall of witnesses when charge altered
description: Whenever a charge is altered or added to by the Court after the commencement of the trial, the prosecutor and the accused shall be allowed to recall or re-summon, and examine with reference to such alteration or addition, any witness who may have been examined.

title: Separate charges for distinct offences
description: For every distinct offence of which any person is accused there shall be a separate charge and every such charge shall be tried separately.

title: Offences of same kind within year may be charged together
description: When a person is accused of more offences than one of the same kind committed within the space of twelve months, he may be charged with, and tried at one trial for, any number of them not exceeding three.
keywords: same kind, twelve months, three offences

title: Trial for more than one offence
description: If, in one series of acts so connected together as to form the same transaction, more offences than one are committed by the same person, he may be charged with, and tried at one trial for, every such offence.

title: Where it is doubtful what offence has been committed
description: If a single act or series of acts is of such a nature that it is doubtful which of several offences the facts will be proved to constitute, the accused may be charged with having committed all or any of such offences.

title: When offence proved included in offence charged
description: When a person is charged with an offence consisting of several particulars, a combination of some only of which constitutes a complete minor offence, and such combination is proved, he may be convicted of the minor offence.

title: What persons may be charged jointly
description: Persons accused of the same offence committed in the course of the same transaction, or of different offences committed in the course of the same transaction, or of more than one offence of the same kind committed within twelve months, may be charged and tried together.
keywords: jointly charged, same transaction, same kind

title: Withdrawal of remaining charges on conviction on one of several charges
description: When a charge containing more heads than one is framed against the same person, and when a conviction has been had on one or more of them, the complainant, or the officer conducting the prosecution, may, with the consent of the Court, withdraw the remaining charge or charges.

title: Trial to be conducted by Public Prosecutor
description: In every trial before a Court of Session, the prosecution shall be conducted by a Public Prosecutor.

title: Opening case for prosecution
description: When the accused appears or is brought before the Court in pursuance of a commitment of the case under section 232, the prosecutor shall open his case by describing the charge brought against the accused.

title: Discharge
description: If, upon consideration of the record of the case and the documents submitted therewith, and after hearing the submissions of the accused and the prosecution, the Judge considers that there is not sufficient ground for proceeding against the accused, he shall discharge the accused and record his reasons for so doing.

title: Framing of charge
description: If, after such consideration and hearing as aforesaid, the Judge is of opinion that there is ground for presuming that the accused has committed the offence, he shall frame in writing a charge against the accused.

title: Conviction on plea of guilty
description: If the accused pleads guilty, the Judge shall record the plea and may, in his discretion, convict him thereon.

title: Date for prosecution evidence
description: If the accused refuses to plead, or does not plead, or claims to be tried or is not convicted under section 252, the Judge shall fix a date for the examination of witnesses, and may issue any process which he deems necessary for compelling the attendance of any witness or the production of any document.

title: Evidence for prosecution
description: On the date so fixed, the Judge shall proceed to take all such evidence as may be produced in support of the prosecution, or the Judge may permit the cross-examination of any witness to be deferred until any other witness has been examined.

title: Acquittal
description: If, after taking the evidence for the prosecution, examining the accused and hearing the prosecution and the defence on the point, the Judge considers that there is no evidence that the accused committed the offence, the Judge shall record an order of acquittal.

title: Entering upon defence
description: Where the accused is not acquitted under section 255, he shall be called upon to enter on his defence and adduce any evidence he may have in support of the same.

title: Arguments
description: When the examination of the witnesses for the defence, if any, is complete, the prosecutor shall sum up his case and the accused or his advocate shall be entitled to reply.

title: Judgment of acquittal or conviction
description: After hearing arguments and points of law, if any, the Judge shall give a judgment in the case.

title: Previous conviction
description: In any case in which a previous conviction is charged under the provisions of this Sanhita, and the accused does not admit that he has been previously convicted, the Judge may inquire into such previous conviction after the judgment of conviction.

title: Procedure in cases instituted under sub-section (2) of section 222
description: In any case instituted under sub-section (2) of section 222, the Court may, instead of requiring the complainant to produce his witnesses, examine them by commission.

title: Compliance with section 230
description: When, in any warrant-case instituted on a police report, the accused appears or is brought before a Magistrate, the Magistrate shall satisfy himself that the accused has been furnished copies as required under section 230.

title: When accused shall be discharged
description: If, upon considering the police report and the documents sent with it and making such examination, if any, of the accused as the Magistrate thinks necessary, the Magistrate considers that the charge is groundless, he shall discharge the accused, and record his reasons for so doing.

title: Framing of charge
description: If, upon such consideration, examination, if any, and hearing, the Magistrate is of opinion that there is ground for presuming that the accused has committed an offence, he shall frame in writing a charge against the accused.

title: Conviction on plea of guilty
description: If the accused pleads guilty, the Magistrate shall record the plea and may, in his discretion, convict him thereon.

title: Evidence for prosecution
description: If the accused refuses to plead, or does not plead, or claims to be tried or the Magistrate does not convict the accused under section 264, the Magistrate shall fix a date for the examination of witnesses.

title: Evidence for defence
description: The accused shall then be called upon to enter upon his defence and produce his evidence; and if the accused puts in any written statement, the Magistrate shall file it with the record.

title: Evidence for prosecution
description: When proceedings are instituted otherwise than on a police report and the accused appears, the Magistrate shall proceed to hear the prosecution and take all such evidence as may be produced in support of the prosecution.

title: When accused shall be discharged
description: If the Magistrate, upon taking all the evidence and considering the same, is of opinion that no case against the accused has been made out which, if unrebutted, would warrant his conviction, the Magistrate shall discharge him.

title: Procedure where accused is not discharged
description: If, when such evidence has been taken or at any previous stage of the case, the Magistrate is of opinion that there is ground for presuming that the accused has committed the offence, he shall frame a charge.

title: Evidence for defence
description: If the accused, after he has entered upon his defence, applies to the Magistrate to issue any process for compelling the attendance of any witness for the purpose of examination or cross-examination, the Magistrate shall issue such process.

title: Acquittal or conviction
description: If, in any case under this Chapter, the Magistrate finds the accused not guilty, he shall record an order of acquittal. If the Magistrate finds the accused guilty, the Magistrate shall pass sentence upon him according to law.

title: Absence of complainant
description: When the proceedings have been instituted upon complaint, and on any day fixed for the hearing of the case, the complainant is absent, the Magistrate may in his discretion either acquit the accused or adjourn the hearing.

title: Compensation for accusation without reasonable cause
description: Whenever any person causes a Magistrate to issue a warrant for the arrest of another person, and it appears that such complaint was made without reasonable cause, the Magistrate may award compensation not exceeding one thousand rupees.

title: Substance of accusation to be stated
description: When in a summons-case the accused appears or is brought before the Magistrate, the particulars of the offence shall be stated to him, and he shall be asked whether he pleads guilty or has any defence to make.

title: Conviction on plea of guilty
description: If the accused pleads guilty, the Magistrate shall record the plea as nearly as possible in the words used by the accused and may, in his discretion, convict him thereon.

title: Conviction on plea of guilty in absence of accused in petty cases
description: Where a summons has been issued under section 229 and the accused has pleaded guilty by post and desires to be convicted and sentenced in his absence, the Magistrate may, in his discretion, convict and sentence him accordingly.

title: Procedure when not convicted
description: If the Magistrate does not convict the accused under section 275 or section 276, the Magistrate shall proceed to hear the prosecution and take all such evidence as may be produced in support of the prosecution, and also to hear the accused and take all such evidence as he produces in his defence.

title: Acquittal or conviction
description: If the Magistrate, upon taking the evidence, finds the accused not guilty, the Magistrate shall record an order of acquittal. If the Magistrate finds the accused guilty, the Magistrate shall pass sentence upon him according to law.

title: Non-appearance or death of complainant
description: If the summons has been issued on complaint, and on the day appointed the complainant does not appear, the Magistrate shall, notwithstanding anything hereinbefore contained, acquit the accused, unless the Magistrate adjourns the case.

title: Withdrawal of complaint
description: If a complainant, at any time before a final order is passed in any case, satisfies the Magistrate that there are sufficient grounds for permitting him to withdraw his complaint against the accused, the Magistrate may permit him to withdraw.

title: Power to stop proceedings in certain cases
description: In any summons-case instituted otherwise than upon complaint, a Magistrate of the first class or any Magistrate empowered by the State Government in this behalf may, for reasons to be recorded by him, stop the proceedings at any stage without pronouncing any judgment.

title: Power of Court to convert summons-cases into warrant-cases
description: When in the course of the trial of a summons-case relating to an offence punishable with imprisonment for a term exceeding six months, it appears to the Magistrate that it is expedient, he may proceed to re-hear the case as a warrant-case.

title: Power to try summarily
description: Notwithstanding anything contained in this Sanhita, any Chief Judicial Magistrate, any Metropolitan Magistrate, or any Magistrate of the first class specially empowered by the High Court, may try in a summary way all or any of the offences specified.

title: Summary trial by Magistrate of second class
description: The High Court may confer on any Magistrate invested with the powers of a Magistrate of the second class power to try summarily any offence which is punishable only with fine or with imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months.

title: Procedure for summary trials
description: In trials under this Chapter, the procedure specified in this Sanhita for the trial of summons-cases shall be followed except as hereinafter provided.

title: Record in summary trials
description: In every case tried summarily, the Magistrate shall enter a brief statement of the substance of the evidence, the particulars of any previous conviction, and the finding, sentence, or other final order.

title: Judgment in cases tried summarily
description: In every case tried summarily in which the accused does not plead guilty, the Magistrate shall record the substance of the evidence and a judgment containing a brief statement of the reasons for the finding.

title: Language of record and judgment
description: Every such record and judgment shall be written in the language of the Court.

title: Application of Chapter
description: This Chapter shall apply to any person accused of an offence other than offences punishable with death or imprisonment for life or imprisonment for a term exceeding seven years.

title: Application for plea bargaining
description: A person accused of an offence may file an application for plea bargaining in the Court in which such offence is pending for trial.

title: Guidelines for mutually satisfactory disposition
description: The Court shall provide time to the prosecutor, the accused and the victim to work out a mutually satisfactory disposition of the case which may include giving to the victim by the accused the compensation and other expenses.

title: Report of mutually satisfactory disposition to be submitted before Court
description: Where a satisfactory disposition of the case has been worked out, the Court shall prepare a report of such disposition which shall be signed by the presiding officer of the Court and all other persons who participated.

title: Disposal of case
description: The Court shall dispose of the case by sentencing the accused to one-fourth of the punishment provided or extendable for such offence.

title: Judgment of Court
description: The Court shall deliver the judgment in terms of the mutually satisfactory disposition including the agreed-upon compensation to the victim.

title: Finality of judgment
description: The judgment delivered by the Court under this Chapter shall be final and no appeal (except the special leave petition under article 136 and writ petition under articles 226 and 227 of the Constitution) shall lie in any Court against such judgment.

title: Power of Court in plea bargaining
description: The Court, in working out a mutually satisfactory disposition, shall follow such procedure as may be prescribed by the State Government.

title: Period of detention undergone by accused to be set off against sentence of imprisonment
description: The period of detention, if any, undergone by the accused shall be set off against the sentence of imprisonment imposed under this Chapter.

title: Savings
description: The provisions of this Chapter shall have effect notwithstanding anything inconsistent therewith contained in any other provisions of this Sanhita.

title: Statements of accused not to be used
description: The statements or facts stated by an accused in an application for plea bargaining shall not be used for any other purpose except for the purpose of this Chapter.

title: Non-application of Chapter
description: Nothing in this Chapter shall apply to any juvenile or child as defined in section 2 of the Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015.

title: Definitions
description: In this Chapter, 'detention' means detention in any jail, prison or other place of confinement; 'prison' includes any place used for the reception or detention of prisoners.

title: Power to require attendance of prisoners
description: Whenever, in the course of an inquiry, trial or other proceeding under this Sanhita, it appears to a Criminal Court that the attendance of a prisoner is necessary for giving evidence or for the purpose of identification, the Court may make an order requiring the officer in charge of the prison to produce such prisoner.

title: Power of State Government or Central Government to exclude certain persons from operation of section 302
description: The State Government or the Central Government may, at any time, having regard to the matters specified, by general or special order, direct that any person or class of persons shall not be removed from the prison in which he or they may be confined.

title: Officer in charge of prison to abstain from carrying out order in certain contingencies
description: Where the person in respect of whom an order is made is certified by the medical officer attached to the prison to be unfit to be removed, the officer in charge of the prison shall abstain from carrying out the Court's order.

title: Prisoner to be brought to Court in custody
description: Any person attending any inquiry, trial or other proceeding under this Sanhita in pursuance of an order made under section 302 shall, during such attendance, be kept in such custody as the Court may direct.

title: Power to issue commission for examination of witness in prison
description: Any Criminal Court may, if it appears to it to be expedient in the interests of justice, issue a commission for the examination of any witness who is confined in a prison.

title: Language of Courts
description: The State Government may determine what shall be the language of each Court for purposes of this Sanhita other than the language of the record.

title: Evidence to be taken in presence of accused
description: Except as otherwise expressly provided, all evidence taken in the course of the trial or other proceeding shall be taken in the presence of the accused, or, when his personal attendance is dispensed with, in the presence of his advocate.

title: Record in summons-cases and inquiries
description: In all summons-cases tried by a Magistrate, and in all inquiries, the Magistrate shall, as the examination of each witness proceeds, make a memorandum of the substance of the evidence.

title: Record in warrant-cases
description: In all warrant-cases tried before a Magistrate, the evidence of each witness shall, as his examination proceeds, be taken down in writing either by the Magistrate himself or by the dictation of the Magistrate directly in the digital device.

title: Record in trial before Court of Session
description: In all trials before a Court of Session, the evidence of each witness shall be taken down in writing by the presiding Judge or in his presence.

title: Language of record of evidence
description: In all cases where the evidence is taken down under sections 309, 310 or 311, it shall be taken down in the language of the Court, or if the Court so directs, the evidence or any particular part thereof shall be taken down in the language in which it is given.

title: Procedure in regard to such evidence when completed
description: As the evidence of each witness taken under sections 309, 310 or 311 is completed, it shall be read over to him in the presence of the accused, if in attendance, or of his advocate, if he appears by advocate.

title: Interpretation of evidence to accused or his advocate
description: Whenever evidence is given in a language not understood by the accused, and he is present in Court in person, it shall be interpreted to him in open Court in a language understood by him.

title: Remarks respecting demeanour of witness
description: The Magistrate or presiding Judge may record such remarks respecting the demeanour of any witness while under examination as he thinks material.

title: Record of examination of accused
description: When the accused is examined by a Court, a memorandum of the substance of his examination shall be made in the language of the Court, signed by the presiding Judge or Magistrate.

title: Interpreter to be bound to interpret truthfully
description: When the services of an interpreter are required by any Criminal Court, he shall be bound to state the true interpretation of the evidence.

title: Record in High Court
description: Every trial before a High Court and every appeal under this Sanhita to a High Court shall be dealt with in accordance with any rules made by the High Court.

title: When attendance of witness may be dispensed with and commission issued
description: Whenever, in the course of any inquiry, trial or other proceeding, the Court is satisfied that the attendance of a witness is not reasonably practicable, or that the delay caused by procuring his attendance would occasion unreasonable delay, the Court may dispense with his attendance and issue a commission for his examination.

title: Commission to whom to be issued
description: A commission for the examination of any witness may be issued to any District Magistrate or Magistrate of the first class, or to any other person if there is sufficient reason.

title: Execution of commissions
description: On receipt of the commission, the person to whom the commission is directed shall summon the witness before him and shall take down his evidence in the same manner as in trials.

title: Parties may examine witnesses
description: The parties to the proceeding may appear before the person executing the commission and examine, cross-examine and re-examine the witness.

title: Return of commission
description: After any commission issued under section 319 has been duly executed, it shall be returned, together with the deposition of the witness, to the Court issuing it.

title: Adjournment of proceeding
description: In every case in which a commission is issued under section 319, the inquiry, trial or other proceeding may be adjourned for a specified time reasonably sufficient.

title: Execution of foreign commissions
description: The provisions of section 321 shall apply in respect of commissions issued by any Court in any country outside India in the same manner as they apply in respect of commissions issued under section 319.

title: Deposition of medical witness
description: The deposition of a civil surgeon or other medical witness taken and attested by a Magistrate in the presence of the accused, or taken on commission, may be given in evidence in any inquiry, trial or other proceeding.

title: Identification report of Magistrate
description: Any document purporting to be a report under the hand of any Magistrate of any proceeding held by him for the purpose of identifying any person may be used as evidence.

title: Evidence of officers of Mint
description: Any document purporting to be signed by an officer of the Mint and stating the result of any assay or examination of any gold or silver bullion shall be admissible in evidence.

title: Reports of certain Government scientific experts
description: Any document purporting to be a report signed by the Director or Deputy Director or Assistant Director of any Government scientific expert's laboratory containing the result of any chemical or scientific examination may be used as evidence.

title: No formal proof of certain documents
description: Where any document purporting to be signed by any officer mentioned in section 328 or 329 is tendered in evidence, the Court shall presume that the signature is genuine and the person signing was holding the official character.

title: Affidavit in proof of conduct of public servants
description: When any application is made to any Court in the course of any inquiry, trial or other proceeding, the application shall be supported by an affidavit.

title: Evidence of formal character on affidavit
description: The evidence of any person whose evidence is of a formal character may be given by affidavit.

title: Authorities before whom affidavits may be sworn
description: Affidavits may be sworn or affirmed before any Judge, Magistrate, or any officer specially authorised to administer oaths.

title: Previous conviction or acquittal how proved
description: In any inquiry, trial or other proceeding, a previous conviction or acquittal may be proved by a certificate signed by the officer having the custody of the records.

title: Record of evidence in absence of accused
description: If it is proved that an accused person has absconded, evidence may be taken in his absence in the same manner as if he were present.

title: Evidence of public servants, experts, police officers in certain cases
description: The evidence of any public servant, expert or police officer given by way of affidavit or through audio-video electronic means shall be admissible.

title: Person once convicted or acquitted not to be tried for same offence
description: A person who has once been tried by a Court of competent jurisdiction for an offence and convicted or acquitted of such offence shall not be liable to be tried again for the same offence.
keywords: double jeopardy, autrefois convict, autrefois acquit

title: Appearance by Public Prosecutors
description: The Public Prosecutor may appear and plead without any written authority before any Court in which that case is under inquiry, trial or appeal.

title: Permission to conduct prosecution
description: Any Magistrate inquiring into or trying a case may permit the prosecution to be conducted by any person other than a police officer below the rank of Inspector, but no person other than the Advocate General or Public Prosecutor shall be entitled to do so without such permission.

title: Right of person against whom proceedings are instituted to be defended
description: Any person accused of an offence before a Criminal Court, or against whom proceedings are instituted under this Sanhita, may of right be defended by an advocate of his choice.

title: Legal aid to accused at State expense in certain cases
description: Where, in a trial before the Court of Session, the accused is not represented by an advocate, and where it appears to the Court that the accused has not sufficient means to engage an advocate, the Court shall assign an advocate for his defence at the expense of the State.

title: Procedure when corporation or registered society is an accused
description: In a prosecution against a corporation or a registered society, the corporation or society may appoint a representative for the purpose of the inquiry or trial.

title: Tender of pardon to accomplice
description: In the case of any offence triable exclusively by the Court of Session or by the Court of a Chief Judicial Magistrate, the Chief Judicial Magistrate may tender a pardon to any person supposed to have been directly or indirectly concerned in or privy to an offence, on condition of his making a full and true disclosure of the whole circumstances.

title: Power to direct tender of pardon
description: At any time after commitment of a case but before judgment is passed, the Court may, with a view to obtaining the evidence of any person supposed to be concerned or privy to the offence, tender a pardon to such person on the condition mentioned.

title: Trial of person not complying with conditions of pardon
description: Where a person who has accepted a tender of pardon fails to comply with the condition on which the tender was made, such person may be tried for the offence in respect of which the pardon was so tendered.

title: Power to postpone or adjourn proceedings
description: Every inquiry and trial may be held at such place, or adjourned from time to time, as the Court may think fit.

title: Local inspection
description: Any Judge or Magistrate may at any stage of any inquiry, trial or other proceeding view any place in or about which any offence is alleged to have been committed.

title: Power to summon material witness, or examine person present
description: Any Court may, at any stage of any inquiry, trial or other proceeding, summon any person as a witness, or recall and re-examine any person already examined.

title: Power of Magistrate to order person to give specimen signatures or handwriting, etc.
description: If a Magistrate of the first class is satisfied that, for the purposes of any investigation or proceeding under this Sanhita, it is expedient to direct any person to give specimen signatures or handwriting, he may make an order to that effect.

title: Expenses of complainants and witnesses
description: Subject to any rules made by the State Government, any Criminal Court may allow to any complainant or witness such reasonable expenses as the Court thinks fit.

title: Power to examine accused
description: The Court may, at any stage of any inquiry or trial, by order in writing, require the production of any document or thing for the purpose of any inquiry or trial.

title: Oral arguments and memorandum of arguments
description: Any party to a proceeding may, as soon as may be, after the close of his evidence, address concise oral arguments, and may, before he concludes the oral arguments, submit a memorandum.

title: Accused person to be competent witness
description: Any person accused of an offence before a Criminal Court shall be a competent witness for the defence and may give evidence on oath in disproof of the charges made against him.

title: No influence to be used to induce disclosure
description: Except as provided in sections 343 and 344, no influence, by means of any promise or threat or otherwise, shall be used to an accused person to induce him to disclose or withhold any matter.

title: Provision for inquiries and trial being held in absence of accused in certain cases
description: At any stage of an inquiry or trial under this Sanhita, if the Judge or Magistrate is satisfied that the personal attendance of the accused is not necessary in the interests of justice, the Judge or Magistrate may dispense with his attendance.

title: Inquiry, trial or judgment in absentia of proclaimed offender
description: When a person declared as a proclaimed offender, whether or not charged jointly with others, has absconded to evade trial and there is no immediate prospect of arresting him, the Court may, in his absence, examine the witnesses and pronounce judgment.

title: Procedure where accused does not understand proceedings
description: If the accused, though not of unsound mind, cannot be made to understand the proceedings, the Court may proceed with the inquiry or trial; and, in the case of a trial, the Court may proceed to judgment.

title: Power to proceed against other persons appearing to be guilty of offence
description: Where, in the course of any inquiry or trial of an offence, it appears from the evidence that any person not being the accused has committed any offence, the Court may proceed against such person for the offence which he appears to have committed.

title: Compounding of offences
description: The offences punishable under the sections of BNS specified in the first and second columns of the Table in this section may be compounded by the persons mentioned in the third column of that Table.

title: Withdrawal from prosecution
description: Any Public Prosecutor may, with the consent of the Court, at any time before the judgment is pronounced, withdraw from the prosecution of any person either generally or in respect of any one or more of the offences for which he is tried.

title: Procedure in cases which Magistrate cannot dispose of
description: If, in the course of a trial before a Magistrate, it appears at any stage of the proceedings that the case is one which should be tried by a higher Court, the Magistrate shall commit the case to the Court of Session.

title: Procedure when after commencement of inquiry or trial, Magistrate finds case should be committed
description: If, in any inquiry or trial before a Magistrate, it appears at any stage of the proceedings that the offence is one which ought to be tried by the Court of Session, the Magistrate shall commit the accused to the Court of Session.

title: Trial of persons previously convicted of offences against coinage, stamp-law or property
description: Where a person, having been convicted of an offence punishable under Chapter XII or Chapter XVIII of BNS, is again accused of any offence punishable under either of those Chapters, the Court may inquire into or try such offence.

title: Procedure when Magistrate cannot pass sentence sufficiently severe
description: Whenever a Magistrate is of opinion, after hearing the evidence for the prosecution and the accused, that the accused is guilty, and that he ought to receive a punishment different in kind from, or more severe than, that which such Magistrate is empowered to inflict, or that he ought to be required to execute a bond, he may record the opinion and submit his proceedings and forward the accused to the Chief Judicial Magistrate.

title: Conviction or commitment on evidence partly recorded by one Magistrate and partly by another
description: Whenever any Magistrate, after having heard and recorded the whole or any part of the evidence in an inquiry or a trial, ceases to exercise jurisdiction therein, and is succeeded by another Magistrate, the succeeding Magistrate may continue the inquiry or trial.

title: Court to be open
description: Subject to provisions for protection of woman and child witnesses, the inquiry and trial shall be held in an open Court, which shall be accessible to the public generally.

title: Procedure in case of accused being person of unsound mind
description: When a Magistrate holding an inquiry has reason to believe that the person against whom the inquiry is being held is of unsound mind and consequently incapable of making his defence, the Magistrate shall inquire into the fact of such unsoundness.

title: Procedure in case of person of unsound mind tried before Court
description: If at the trial of any person before a Magistrate or Court of Session, it appears to the Magistrate or Court that such person is of unsound mind and consequently incapable of making his defence, the Magistrate or Court shall, in the first instance, try the fact of such unsoundness and incapacity.

title: Release of person of unsound mind pending investigation or trial
description: Whenever a person is found to be of unsound mind and incapable of making his defence, the Magistrate or Court shall order such person to be detained in safe custody in such place and manner as the Magistrate or Court thinks fit.

title: Resumption of inquiry or trial
description: Whenever an inquiry or a trial is postponed under section 367 or section 368, the Magistrate or Court may at any time resume the inquiry or trial, and require the accused to appear or be brought before such Magistrate or Court.

title: Procedure on accused appearing before Magistrate or Court
description: When the accused appears or is again brought before the Magistrate or Court, the Magistrate or Court shall, if such Magistrate or Court considers the accused capable of making his defence, proceed with the inquiry or trial.

title: When accused appears to have been of sound mind
description: When the accused appears to be of sound mind at the time of inquiry or trial, the Magistrate or Court shall proceed with the case.

title: Judgment of acquittal on ground of unsoundness of mind
description: When any person is acquitted on the ground that, at the time of the commission of the act alleged to have been committed by him he was, by reason of unsoundness of mind, incapable of knowing the nature of the act, or that it was wrong or contrary to law, the finding shall state specifically whether he committed the act or not.

title: Person acquitted on ground of unsoundness of mind to be detained in safe custody
description: Whenever the finding states that the accused committed the act, the Magistrate or Court shall order such person to be detained in safe custody in such place and manner as the Magistrate or Court thinks fit.

title: Power of State Government to empower officer in charge to discharge
description: The State Government may empower the officer in charge of the jail in which a person is confined under section 369 or section 374 to discharge all or any of the persons so confined.

title: Procedure where prisoner of unsound mind is reported capable of making his defence
description: If any person detained under section 369 is, in the opinion of the officer in charge of the jail where he is detained, capable of making his defence, the officer shall report the matter to the Court.

title: Procedure where person of unsound mind detained is declared fit to be released
description: If any person detained under section 374 is, in the judgment of two or more medical officers, fit to be released, such officer shall report the fact to the State Government.
keywords: fit to release, medical officers, State Government

title: Delivery of person of unsound mind to care of relative or friend
description: Whenever any relative or friend of any person detained under section 369 or section 374 desires that he shall be delivered to his care and custody, the State Government may order the same.

title: Procedure in cases mentioned in section 215
description: When any Civil, Revenue or Criminal Court is of opinion that it is expedient in the interests of justice that an inquiry should be made into any offence referred to in section 215, such Court may send a complaint in writing to a Magistrate of the first class having jurisdiction.

title: Appeal
description: Any person convicted of any offence mentioned in section 215 may appeal from such conviction to the Court to which decrees or orders made in such Court are ordinarily appealable.

title: Power to order costs
description: Any Court dealing with an application made to it under section 379 or section 380 shall have power to award such costs as it may consider reasonable to any of the parties to such application.

title: Procedure of Magistrate taking cognizance
description: When a complaint is made to a Magistrate under section 379, he shall inquire into the matter and issue process in the manner provided for in the case of complaints.

title: Summary procedure for trial for giving false evidence
description: If a person appearing as a witness before any Criminal Court is charged with giving false evidence, the Court may, if satisfied that it is necessary in the interests of justice, take cognizance of the offence and try such person summarily.
punishment: Summary trial for perjury

title: Procedure in certain cases of contempt
description: When any such offence as is described in section 217, 218 or 219 of BNS is committed in the view or presence of any Civil, Revenue or Criminal Court, the Court may cause the offender to be detained in custody and may take cognizance of the offence.

title: Procedure where Court considers that case should not be dealt with under section 384
description: If the Court considers that the case should not be dealt with under section 384, the Court shall refer the case to a Magistrate having jurisdiction to try the same.

title: When Registrar or Sub-Registrar to be deemed a Civil Court
description: When the State Government so directs, a Registrar or a Sub-Registrar appointed under the Registration Act, 1908 shall be deemed to be a Civil Court within the meaning of section 384.

title: Discharge of offender on submission of apology
description: When any Court has taken cognizance of an offence under section 384, it may, in its discretion, discharge the offender on his submission to the satisfaction of the Court and on payment of such fine as the Court may impose.

title: Imprisonment or committal of person refusing to answer or produce document
description: If any witness or person called to produce a document or thing before a Criminal Court refuses to answer such questions as are put to him or to produce any document or thing in his possession, the Court may commit him to custody.
punishment: Detention until compliance

title: Summary procedure for punishment for non-attendance by a witness in obedience to summons
description: If any witness being summoned to give evidence or to produce a document in a Criminal Court fails to attend, the Court may, after proof of service of the summons, impose upon him such fine not exceeding five hundred rupees.
punishment: Fine up to Rs. 500

title: Appeals from convictions under sections 383, 384, 388 and 389
description: Any person convicted of an offence under section 383 or section 384 or punished under section 388 or section 389 may appeal to the Court to which decrees or orders made in such Court are ordinarily appealable.

title: Certain Judges and Magistrates not to try certain offences when committed before themselves
description: Except as provided in sections 383, 384 and 388, no Judge of a Criminal Court (other than a Judge of a High Court) or Magistrate shall try any person for any offence committed in his view or presence in such Court.
keywords: Judge, Magistrate, not to try, own view

title: Judgment
description: The judgment in every trial in any Criminal Court of original jurisdiction shall be pronounced in open Court by the presiding officer immediately after the termination of the trial, or at some subsequent time of which notice shall be given to the parties or their advocates.

title: Language and contents of judgment
description: Every such judgment shall be written in the language of the Court and shall contain the point or points for determination, the decision thereon and the reasons for the decision.

title: Order for notifying address of previously convicted offender
description: When any person, having been convicted of an offence punishable under Chapter XVII of BNS or under Chapter XII of that Sanhita, is again convicted of any offence punishable under either of those Chapters, the Court convicting him may require him to notify his residence and any change of residence.

title: Order to pay compensation
description: When a Court imposes a sentence of fine or a sentence of which fine forms a part, the Court may, when passing judgment, order the whole or any part of the fine recovered to be applied in the payment to any person of compensation for any loss or injury caused by the offence.

title: Victim compensation scheme
description: Every State Government in co-ordination with the Central Government shall prepare a scheme for providing funds for the purpose of compensation to the victim or his dependents who have suffered loss or injury as a result of the crime.

title: Treatment of victims
description: All hospitals, public or private, whether run by the Central Government, the State Government, local bodies or any other person, shall immediately provide the first-aid or medical treatment, free of cost, to the victims of any offence covered under section 64, section 65, section 66 or section 67 of BNS.
keywords: victim treatment, hospital, free of cost

title: Witness protection scheme
description: Every State Government shall prepare and notify a witness protection scheme for the State with a view to ensure protection of the witnesses.

title: Compensation to persons groundlessly arrested
description: Whenever any person causes the arrest of another person, if it appears to the Magistrate by whom the case is heard that there was no sufficient ground for causing such arrest, the Magistrate may award to the person so arrested a sum not exceeding one thousand rupees.
punishment: Compensation up to Rs. 1000

title: Order to pay costs in non-cognizable cases
description: Whenever any complaint of a non-cognizable offence is made to a Court, the Court, if it convicts the accused, may, while passing judgment, order the accused to pay to the complainant, in whole or in part, the cost incurred by him in the prosecution.

title: Order to release on probation of good conduct or after admonition
description: When any person is convicted of an offence punishable with imprisonment for a term not exceeding seven years or with fine, and it appears to the Court that regard to character, antecedents, age, health, or mental condition, it is expedient to release him on probation.

title: Special reasons to be recorded in certain cases
description: When the court does not pass the minimum sentence specified for an offence, it shall record in writing special and adequate reasons for awarding lesser sentence.

title: Court not to alter judgment
description: Save as otherwise provided by this Sanhita or by any other law, no Court, when it has signed its judgment or final order disposing of a case, shall alter or review the same except to correct a clerical or arithmetical error.

title: Copy of judgment to be given to accused and other persons
description: When the accused is sentenced to imprisonment, a copy of the judgment shall be immediately given to him free of cost. Copy of judgment shall also be given to victim or dependent free of cost.

title: Judgment when to be translated
description: When a judgment is written in a language different from that in which it was delivered, it shall be translated into the language in which it was delivered.

title: Court of Session to send copy of finding and sentence to District Magistrate
description: In cases tried by the Court of Session or a Chief Judicial Magistrate, the Court or such Magistrate shall send a copy of the finding and sentence to the District Magistrate within whose jurisdiction the trial was held.

title: Sentence of death to be submitted by Court of Session for confirmation
description: When the Court of Session passes a sentence of death, the proceedings shall be submitted to the High Court, and the sentence shall not be executed unless confirmed by the High Court.

title: Power to direct further inquiry to be made or additional evidence to be taken
description: The High Court may direct further inquiry to be made into any alleged offence, or additional evidence to be taken, and may give other directions as it thinks fit.
keywords: further inquiry, additional evidence, High Court

title: Power of High Court to confirm sentence or annul conviction
description: In any case submitted under section 407, the High Court may confirm the sentence, or pass any other sentence warranted by law, or annul the conviction.

title: Confirmation or new sentence to be signed by two Judges
description: No order of confirmation shall be made under this Chapter and no new sentence shall be passed unless such order or sentence is agreed to by two Judges of the High Court.

title: Procedure in case of difference of opinion
description: Where any such case is heard by two Judges of the High Court and such Judges are equally divided in opinion, the case shall be laid before another Judge, and the judgment shall follow the opinion of the majority.

title: Procedure in cases submitted to High Court for confirmation
description: In cases submitted by the Court of Session to the High Court for the confirmation of a sentence of death, the proper officer of the High Court shall forthwith deliver the case to the appropriate bench or Judge.

title: No appeal to lie unless otherwise provided
description: No appeal shall lie from any judgment or order of a Criminal Court except as provided for by this Sanhita or by any other law for the time being in force.

title: Appeal from orders requiring security or refusal to accept or rejecting surety for keeping peace or good behaviour
description: Any person who has been ordered to give security for keeping the peace or for good behaviour may appeal against such order.

title: Appeals from convictions
description: Any person convicted on a trial may appeal to the Court of Session if convicted by a Magistrate; or to the High Court if convicted by the Court of Session or Additional Sessions Judge.
keywords: appeal, conviction, Sessions Court, High Court

title: No appeal in certain cases when accused pleads guilty
description: Notwithstanding anything contained in section 415, where an accused person has pleaded guilty and has been convicted on such plea, there shall be no appeal except as to the extent or legality of the sentence.

title: No appeal in petty cases
description: Notwithstanding anything contained in section 415, there shall be no appeal by a convicted person in any case in which a Magistrate passes only a sentence of imprisonment not exceeding three months, or fine not exceeding ten thousand rupees, or both.
keywords: no appeal, petty cases, three months

title: Appeal by State Government against sentence
description: The State Government may, in any case, direct the Public Prosecutor to present an appeal to the High Court against the sentence on the ground of its inadequacy.

title: Appeal in case of acquittal
description: The State Government may direct the Public Prosecutor to present an appeal to the High Court from an order of acquittal passed by any Court other than a High Court.

title: Appeal against conviction by High Court in certain cases
description: Where the High Court has, on appeal, reversed an order of acquittal of an accused person and convicted him and sentenced him to death or to imprisonment for life or to imprisonment for a term of ten years or more, he may appeal to the Supreme Court.
keywords: appeal, Supreme Court, High Court reversal

title: Special right of appeal in certain cases
description: Notwithstanding anything contained in this Chapter, when more persons than one are convicted in one trial, and an appealable judgment or order has been passed in respect of any of such persons, all or any of the persons so convicted may appeal.

title: Appeal to Court of Session how heard
description: Subject to the provisions of this Sanhita, an appeal to the Court of Session or Sessions Judge shall be heard by the Sessions Judge or an Additional Sessions Judge.

title: Petition of appeal
description: Every appeal shall be made in the form of a petition in writing presented by the appellant or his advocate, and every such petition shall be accompanied by a copy of the judgment or order appealed against.

title: Procedure when appellant in jail
description: If the appellant is in jail, he may present his petition of appeal and the copies accompanying the same to the officer in charge of the jail, who shall thereupon forward such petition and copies to the proper Appellate Court.

title: Summary dismissal of appeal
description: If upon examining the petition of appeal and copy of the judgment received under section 423 or 424, the Appellate Court considers that there is no sufficient ground for interfering, it may dismiss the appeal summarily.

title: Procedure for hearing appeals not dismissed summarily
description: If the Appellate Court does not dismiss the appeal summarily, it shall cause notice of the time and place at which such appeal will be heard to be given to the appellant and the Public Prosecutor.

title: Powers of Appellate Court
description: The Appellate Court shall have power to dismiss the appeal, or to allow it and acquit the accused, or alter the finding while maintaining the sentence, or alter the nature or extent of punishment.

title: Judgments of subordinate Appellate Court
description: The rules contained in Chapter XXVII as to judgments of Criminal Courts of original jurisdiction shall apply to the judgments of Courts of Appeal other than the High Court.

title: Order of High Court on appeal to be certified to lower Court
description: Whenever a case is decided on appeal by the High Court, it shall certify its judgment or order to the Court by which the finding, sentence or order appealed against was recorded or passed.

title: Suspension of sentence pending appeal; release of appellant on bail
description: Pending any appeal by a convicted person, the Appellate Court may order that the execution of the sentence be suspended and also, if he is in confinement, that he be released on bail or on his own bond.

title: Arrest of accused in appeal from acquittal
description: When an appeal is presented under section 419 against an acquittal, the High Court may issue a warrant directing that the accused be arrested and brought before it.

title: Appellate Court may take further evidence or direct it to be taken
description: In dealing with any appeal under this Chapter, the Appellate Court, if it thinks additional evidence to be necessary, may take such evidence or direct it to be taken by the trial Court.

title: Procedure where Judges of Court of Appeal are equally divided
description: When the Judges of the Court of Appeal are equally divided in opinion, the case shall be laid before another Judge of the same Court, and such Judge shall hear the appeal and deliver his opinion, and the judgment or order shall follow such opinion.

title: Finality of judgments and orders on appeal
description: Judgments and orders passed by an Appellate Court upon appeal shall be final, except in the cases provided for in section 420 and Chapter XXX.

title: Abatement of appeals
description: Every appeal under section 415 or section 419 shall finally abate on the death of the accused, and every other appeal under this Chapter shall finally abate on the death of the appellant.

title: Reference to High Court
description: When any Court is satisfied that a case pending before it involves a question as to the validity of any Act, Ordinance, Regulation or of any provision contained in such law, it shall state a case setting forth its opinion and refer it for the decision of the High Court.

title: Disposal of case according to decision of High Court
description: When a case has been referred to the High Court under section 436, the Court by which it was referred shall dispose of the case in conformity with the decision of the High Court.

title: Calling for records to exercise powers of revision
description: The High Court or any Sessions Judge may call for and examine the record of any proceeding before any inferior Criminal Court situate within its or his local jurisdiction for the purpose of satisfying itself or himself as to the correctness, legality or propriety of any finding, sentence or order.
keywords: revision, records, High Court, Sessions Judge

title: Power to order inquiry
description: On examining any record under section 438, or otherwise, the High Court or the Sessions Judge may direct the District Magistrate to make, or may make himself, an inquiry into any complaint against any inferior Court situate within its or his local jurisdiction.

title: Sessions Judge's powers of revision
description: The Sessions Judge may exercise the powers of revision in respect of any proceeding before any Magistrate under his jurisdiction. Such powers shall be subject to the superintendence and control of the High Court.

title: Power of Additional Sessions Judge
description: An Additional Sessions Judge shall have and may exercise all the powers of a Sessions Judge under this Chapter in respect of any case which may be transferred to him by the Sessions Judge.

title: High Court's powers of revision
description: The High Court may exercise its powers of revision: to satisfy itself as to the correctness, legality or propriety of any finding, sentence or order; as to the regularity of any proceedings.

title: Power of High Court to withdraw or transfer revision cases
description: Whenever one or more persons convicted at the same trial makes or make an application to a Sessions Judge for revision and another or other of such convicted persons makes or make an application to the High Court for revision, the High Court shall decide which Court shall hear the applications.

title: Option of Court to hear parties
description: Save as otherwise expressly provided by this Sanhita, no party has any right to be heard either personally or by advocate before any Court exercising its powers of revision.

title: High Court's order to be certified to lower Court
description: When a case is revised by the High Court, it shall certify its decision or order to the Court by which the finding, sentence or order revised was recorded or passed.

title: Power of Supreme Court to transfer cases and appeals
description: Whenever it is made to appear to the Supreme Court that an order under this section is expedient for the ends of justice, it may direct that any particular case or appeal be transferred from one High Court to another High Court.

title: Power of High Court to transfer cases and appeals
description: Whenever it is made to appear to the High Court that an order under this section is expedient for the ends of justice, it may direct that any particular case be transferred from one Criminal Court subordinate to it to another.

title: Power of Sessions Judge to transfer cases and appeals
description: Whenever it is made to appear to a Sessions Judge that an order under this section is expedient for the ends of justice, he may direct that any particular case be transferred from one Criminal Court to another Criminal Court in his sessions division.

title: Withdrawal of cases and appeals by Sessions Judge
description: A Sessions Judge may withdraw any case or appeal from, or recall any case or appeal which he has made over to, any Assistant Sessions Judge or Chief Judicial Magistrate subordinate to him.

title: Withdrawal of cases by Judicial Magistrate
description: Any Chief Judicial Magistrate may withdraw any case from, or recall any case which he has made over to, any Magistrate subordinate to him, and may inquire into or try such case himself, or refer it for inquiry or trial to any other such Magistrate.

title: Making over or withdrawal of cases by Executive Magistrates
description: Any District Magistrate or Sub-divisional Magistrate may withdraw any case from, or recall any case which he has made over to, any Magistrate subordinate to him, and may inquire into or try such case himself, or refer it for inquiry or trial to any other such Magistrate.

title: Reasons to be recorded
description: A Sessions Judge or Magistrate making an order under section 448, 449, 450, or 451 shall record his reasons for making it.

title: Execution of order passed under section 409
